description.name: gaiasense
description.title: An innovative, multi-dimensional smart farming system
description.description:
Gaiasense, an innovative smart farming system designed and developed by NEUROPUBLIC S.A., collects data from the field (atmospheric and soil data), the satellite (Earth Observation data), the scientist (on farm observations, measurements and sampling), and the farmer (recording of farming activities). This data feeds scientific models for the fertilization, irrigation and crop protection of each crop, adjusted to the microclimatic conditions of each site. Then, gaiasense provides advice to agronomists and consequently farmers, so that they can meet the exact needs of each specific crop in terms of nutrients, irrigation water and crop protection measures.

Gaiasense supports a constantly growing number of crops, both open field and in greenhouses, conventional and organic. Its modular design allows the adaptation to different crops and adjustment to different microclimatic conditions. It contributes to better quality and higher yields, with reduced production cost and lower environmental footprint.

Agricultural advisors play a key role in the process, as they are the ones who evaluate the smart farming advice, adjusting it accordingly based on their experience, and then share it with farmers, also guiding them in the application of the advice in the field.

Gaiasense provides agricultural advisors with valuable digital tools, data and information that they need in order to provide more accurate advice to farmers, so that they can reap the benefits of smart farming without requiring any kind of investment in expensive technological equipment nor specialized digital skills.

benefits: Labour saving / Limit stress / Increase farmer’s leisure time

description.description:
Farm Manager is the e-service for business planning, business modelling and decision-making on farms, based on a technically advanced e-service. It is a modern upgrade of the catalogue of calculations, which has historically been provided in the printed version, and has been an indispensable tool for planning business operations on farms for various purposes within the framework of agricultural consulting since 1995. The service enables users to review existing calculations and create farm production plans while advanced users (advisors) can develop and prepare new calculations.
The Farm manager is, in the next iteration going to be upgraded into a data-driven platform, aggregating data received from satellites (Earth Observation), drones, in-situ sensors (IoT), weather stations, soil and plant test databases and other third-party/open data sources.

description.name: SATIVUM
description.title: Satellite monitoring with preloaded parcel boundaries, climate and soil data. Nutrient and pest advises. Communication with the administration.
description.description:
SATIVUM (https://www.sativum.es/) is a free public tool, created by Instituto Tecnológico Agrario de Castilla y León (ITACyL), which aims to help farmers and advisors in the decision making process of a farm, providing them with access to all available information related to their plots through a simple and intuitive web interface from any of their devices: mobile, tablet or computer. 

It is a web app that allows to control of each plot of a farm, carrying out satellite monitoring of the development of crops on pre-loaded and/or editable geometries. It also provides the farmer with information on the climate and soil of his plots, allowing temporal and geographical comparisons to be made on the evolution of crops, as well as zoning the plot for VRA (Variable Rate Application) applications. It also advises on the formulation of nutrient plans (based on FAST tool) and on the possible occurrence of pests and diseases. It allows the completion of the digital farmbook and communication with the administration.

description.name: VegSyst-DSS
description.title: A decision support system for the management of irrigation and nitrogen fertilization in greenhouse vegetable crops
description.description:
VegSyst-DSS is a software developed to calculate daily N fertilizer and irrigation requirements, and the N concentration of the applied nutrient solution applied for fertigated vegetable crops grown in greenhouses. N fertilizer requirements are based on daily crop N uptake and consider soil mineral N at planting, and N mineralized from manure and soil organic matter. Irrigation requirements are based on estimated evapotranspiration (ETc) and consider irrigation application efficiency and the salinity of irrigation water.  ETc can be calculated using the Penman-Monteith equation adapted to greenhouses or the Almeria radiation equation.

VegSyst-DSS has very few inputs, all of which are readily available to farmers and advisors. Data inputs are:
- Daily maximum and minimum air temperature and relative humidity (RH) in the greenhouse, and solar radiation outside the greenhouse.
- The amount of soil mineral N in the root zone at planting.
- Details of the most recent manure application.
- Irrigation layout.
- Soil characteristics.

For the climate data, an internal database of long term average climate data for Almeria can be used; there is no requirement to enter climatic data. Recommendations based on long term average climate data are suitable for climates with very low climatic variation between years such as within greenhouses in Almeria. VegSyst DSS provides a detailed science-based plan of daily N and irrigation requirements that is specific to the characteristics of an individual crop.

VegSyst-DSS is based on the Veg-Syst crop simulation model which calculates daily crop biomass production, critical N uptake and ETc for vegetable crops. The simulation model is driven by thermal time and is adaptable to the variations in climatic conditions thereby providing N and irrigation recommendations specific to given cropping conditions such as season, time of planting, and greenhouse design and covering materials. The VegSyst-DSS assumes that crops have no water or nutrient limitations, which is almost always the case with commercial vegetable production in greenhouses.

AGRIVI Farm Management Solution is a Cloud platform for efficient management of agronomic and business processes. Create a digital copy of the farm for planning and management of field operations, advisory services, intelligent weather data, smart alarms for pests and diseases, traceability and environmental reporting, supply chain management, resources, and finance management, all in one place.
The platform supports growers, cooperatives, agribusinesses, input manufacturers, food processing companies, and other stakeholders who are interested in the management of their crop production or collaboration with growers in their eco-system through agronomic advisory and big-data insights.
The platform is available through the web application and mobile applications with offline mode. 

Multi-Farm access: 	
-	Managing multiple farms in one place 
-	Monitor separate farms and production-related information
Communication platform and In-platform messages: 
-	Seamless communication between individuals or groups, with option to create discussions
-	Push notifications on the platform and the email.
-	Farm Management processes and activity planning	
-	Complete support for farm management activities (plan, track, analyze) for crop production, including all crops and tracking of all activities (work orders):
o	Planning of the Harvest and crop circle management
o	Agronomy visit
o	Scouting with Geo-mapping and positioning
o	Employee and Labor management
o	Land/plot yield management
o	Application of fertilizers with nutrients level
o	Application of protection products with active substances level
o	Equipment usage tracking and management
o	Pest and disease alarm system
Budgeting overview:
-	Detailed forecast planning with all financial data in one place in real-time 
-	Possibility to monitor budget vs. actual spending

description.name: Vetwerk
description.title: Vetwerk software for veterinary cow and herd health support on dairy farms
description.description:
Vetwerk is a software package for veterinarians, that they can use in cow and herd health support on dairy farms. It consists of a website and and two apps. Vetwerk exchanges data with the farm management system of the dairy farm. Veterinarians have access to standardized animal health and production data from the farm and individual animals. 
Vetwerk presents overviews of process data (production, animal health), and work lists, such as a 'cows to check list' for the vet. The veterinarian can adapt the settings, and so taylorize his herd health support services to each individual farm.
In the barn, the vet uses an app, with the list of cows to check, additional information per individual cow, and functions to register findings, remarks, and treatments.
Vetwerk facilitates and assures the structure of the cow and herd health support services, and thus enables the veterinarian to pay attention at the right moment to the right animals and processes, with the relevant supportive data. With this tool, veterinarians can monitor animal health and production processes pro-actively, and early detect issues and problems.

description.name: Dashboard of the peninsular hydrological bulletin.
description.title: The information generated in the weekly Hydrological Bulletin will be able to be viewed through a dashboard-type application
description.description:
The information will be available in a more visual, simple and understandable way, divided into 7 tabs that can be selected at the bottom. In the first 4 you can consult the data of:

- Water reserve: weekly data on the state of peninsular reservoirs larger than 5 hm3.
- Energy: the energy stored in a certain reservoir, the result of the sum of its own energy and repercussion energy.
- Average weekly flows: data on average weekly flows at the gauging stations in the main rivers included in the Hydrological Bulletin.
- Weekly accumulated rainfall: weekly accumulated rainfall data for the current year in a selection of automatic rainfall stations from the Hydrological Bulletin (AEMET data).

Additionally, there are 3 more tabs:
- Help: information about the application and help for data consultation
- Notices: current notices and incidents on the Bulletin data are displayed.
- Contact: shows the way to contact in case of doubts, suggestions or information.

description.name: MILEOS
description.title: Decision Support Tools to control mildew on potatoes
description.description:
The Mileos® tool results from the merging of Mildi-LIS® (ARVALIS - Institut du végétal) and MilPV (SRAL of the French Ministry of Agriculture, Food and Forestry).

a tool predicting the risk of mildew development on potatoes, according to the weather, the variety, the planting and emergence dates, the sanitary state around the plot and the interventions carried out (treatments and irrigation). 

It uses local weather data, recorded by farmer's weather station or from the network of farms advisory structures. 
The farmer creates his plots at the beginning of the season, and inform his crop operations (fungicide treatments and irrigation). He is then informed of the risks of mildew development and intervention advice for each of his plots on the Mileos® website, as well as by text message.

The farmer or his advisor gets a  plot-based decision support tool to position the interventions against mildew.

description.name: Shelt-air
description.title: The Shelt-air diagnostic tool makes it very easy to choose the technical solutions for good natural ventilation of a ruminant breeding building.
description.description:
In a livestock building, the air is charged with water vapor, gas, microorganisms and dust. To maintain a healthy atmosphere and preserve the health of animals, it is important to eliminate these elements by regularly renewing the air in the building. Quality air will also be beneficial for the health of those who work there and for the sustainability of livestock facilities and equipment.
Using the wind to renew the air in buildings means benefiting from a free resource! To get the most out of it, you have to think carefully about the orientation and design of the buildings.

Stemming from the collaboration between IDELE and the company Eternit, the objective of Shelt-air is to correctly size the ventilating openings (in long pan and in ridge) and to choose well its materials of cladding, according the type and number of animals housed, the environment of the building more or less exposed to the wind and the volume of the building.
Calculations are done very quickly either from a computer, tablet or smartphone.

description.name: OPTIPROTECT
description.title: Optiprotect is an easy-to-use and reliable Decision Support Tool (DST) that allows you to better defend your wheat crops.
description.description:
With Optiprotect, protect your wheat crops from diseases and avoid crop losses
Optiprotect is an easy-to-use and reliable Decision Support Tool (DST) that allows you to better defend your wheat crops. Thanks to a precise monitoring of the growth stages of your crops and the risks of fungal diseases, you use the right doses of phytosanitary products, in the right place and at the right time, for a more sustainable agriculture.
Optiprotect values your operating data from MesParcelles whether you are a subscriber or not. If you are not subscribed to MesParcelles, an advisory right is created for an exclusive use of the tool. The uploading of your data is done automatically without the need to re-enter it.
How does the tool work?
Your customized parcel data from MesParcelles are cross-referenced with the spatialized climatological data from Météo France station network. Arvalis-Institut du Végétal's algorithmic models thus predict the dates on which the growth stages of your crops will be reached (ear 1 cm, 1 node, 2 nodes, Last spread leaf - DFE, heading, flowering) and the optimal dates for observation and phytosanitary treatment against the main wheat pests (Downy Mildew, Septoria, Yellow Rust, Brown Rust and Fusarium).
The Optiprotect interface, accessible from your tablet or computer, takes the form of a simple and effective dashboard. Thanks to a specific color code, it allows you to quickly read the evolution of stages and disease risks for all your wheat plots.
You have the possibility to adjust your local weather data in case of very localised bad weather.
Your crops are defended and your interventions secured.
Thanks to Optiprotect you receive:
- Weekly newsletters by e-mail concerning the sanitary state of each of your plots of land.
- Customized notifications on your dashboard
- SMS alerts in advance of key dates for phytosanitary treatments.

By telephone or in the field, your adviser is at your disposal throughout the campaign, to inform you about local illnesses, the correct dosages to be carried out or the adaptation of the treatment day.

Finally for an integrated protection of your crops year after year, Optiprotect offers you the possibility to make a global and technical analysis at the end of your campaigns and to automatically save the history to compare several campaigns.

benefits: Labour saving / Limit stress / Increase farmer’s leisure time

description.name: Pest monitoring
description.title: Monitoring of pests of cereals, oilseed rape, field bean and field peas.
description.description: The monitoring system provides information on the phytosanitary status of the main crops during the growing season and provides regional recommendations for disease and pest control. Monitoring of pests begins with winter crops in May, spring crops are added in June and ends in July. Weekly route monitoring collect data on the prevalence of pests on most cultivated crops. The observation data is presented operatively as a visualized map as an ArcGIS web application with recommendations on the need to use chemical control. To detect early infection, monitoring involves collecting plant samples from visually healthy fields for the early detection of some fungal pathogens (septoria, ramularia) by PCR assays. During the monitoring, beetles are collected from rapeseed fields and the possible presence of insecticide resistance is analyzed. Cereal samples are collected before and during the harvest to determine the presence of Fusarium fungi and mycotoxins.

description.name: SYSTERRE®
description.title: Measuring performances of cropping systems to choose the best suited farming strategies
description.description:
SYSTERRE® strategic choice tool provides key performance indicators at farm level to help farmers define the best suited farming strategies.

How:  By using standardised and scientifically robust calculation methods that are the core of this multi-criteria evaluation tool. Users will be able to precisely describe the functioning of a cropping system and assess the impact of individual choices in real-time. All the calculations made by the tool will be available as Application Programming Interface (API) to allow interconnections between several innovative services.

In 2022, the ergonomics of the final web tool interfaces have been optimised for a more intuitive, efficient and secure navigation.

description.name: SMART Sustainability Monitoring and Assessment RouTine
description.title: SMART is a method that allows farms and companies in the food sector to assess their sustainability in a credible, transparent and comparable manner.
description.description: SMART is an innovative instrument for holistic sustainability analysis and evaluation of agricultural and food companies and is based on the globally valid SAFA guidelines (Sustainability Assessment of Food and Agriculture Systems, FAO 2014). It was developed by more than 60 agricultural and sustainability experts and consists of a highly efficient database as well as a scientifically sound evaluation methodology including an extensive indicator pool. The assessment method involves, among other things, a weighting of the indicators according to the level of impact on the various SAFA subthemes. Furthermore, the sphere of influence and responsibility of the respective farm or company as well as the time, place and responsible party of sustainability impacts within the supply chain are being considered. SMART is not a new standard, certification system or label but solely an analysis and assessment instrument. It therefore does not compete with existing standards and certificates but is a useful supplement. The SMART tool should be implemented by experts, and therefore anyone interested in the tool should contact those responsible for the tool to arrange a training.
keywords: farm level and supply chain sustainability assessment

description.name: Zoom
description.title: Zoom is a free cloud based video conferencing service that advisors can use to virtually meet their clients, either by video or audio only, or both.
description.description:
Many advisory services operate where the rural population is dispersed which represents numerous logistical obstacles that can hinder engagement with farmers. Online video call capabilities, such as those offered by Zoom, are especially useful to advisors who want to engage with their clients virtually. Zoom is a free video chatting app; on a video call, advisors can see a thumbnail video of each participant. Advisors can also share their screen, send text messages to the group and share files and images.

Zoom’s virtual video conferencing solutions can assist agricultural advisors in the following ways:
- Reach: Zoom knows no geographical bounds. It can be used to bring advisors and farmers together despite of their physical distance from one another. 
-Time saving: Advisors and/or farmers do not need to lose time to travel to one another. 
-Motivation: Zooms virtual instant messaging service allows advisors the opportunity to continually motivate their clients.
-Interoperability: Zoom works on all platforms and devices meaning that it is accessible to all. Users who don't have the application, or who don't have internet access, can phone into calls using a predefined phone number. 
-Training and Education: Webinars can be provided that can enable and promote knowledge exchange between advisors and farmers. Furthermore, Zoom Rooms offers participants in workshops the chance to go into breakout sessions. Zoom offers audio as well as video capabilities. Recorded sessions can be viewed later by those who are unable to attend. 
-Zoom is very easy to use; advisors can just share a link to a meeting. Clients only need to click on the link to participate in the meeting. Meetings can also be recorded and therefore shared.

benefits: Better interaction between farmer and advisor

description.name: DigiFarm
description.title: DigiFarm - Integrating clinical and farm management records
description.description:
DigiFarm is a new web-based solution, developed & managed by Kingshay for VetPartners vets, vet staff and farm clients, which integrates clinical and farm management records.
DigiFarm brings together milk records and health data to create unique reports that can be used by both vets and farmers. Sharing data in this way allows a collaborative approach which creates joined up thinking between the whole farm team and their advisers.
In turn, this promotes real-time on-farm proactive discussions and informs management decisions that help improve herd health, increase sustainability, and build business resilience.
The data can also be used to benchmark individual farm businesses against others. This helps highlight strengths and weaknesses and allows efforts to be focussed on areas with the greatest potential for improvement. 
In addition, the storing of records in a structured format reduces the time and effort required by farmers to meet the increasing demands from milk processors, farm assurance and other schemes.

description.name: CropManager
description.title: CropManager is for integrated nutrient management and all other relevant tasks for professional crop management.
description.description:
General description
CropManager is available online from the web address https://www.cropmanager.eu. The tool is with a dynamic layout optimised for use and installation as an individual app on all devices, independent of screen resolutions. The app includes the following elements:

Enterprise level
•	Management of livestock manures and other organic fertilisers, include nutrient accounting and budgeting, sales and purchases.
•	Registration of chemicals purchases.
•	Printing of a variety of reports, including printouts with the main aim of supporting crop management, and printouts to serve as documentation to authorities. All printouts are available in PDF formats, and a few also in EXCEL format.

Production site level
•	Soil analyses can be registered with various parameters, including geographical coordinates.
•	Planning of crop rotation, use of seeds, irrigation, fertilisation, and crop protection.
•	The actual performed field operations, such as sowing, field spreading of fertilisers, harvesting etc. can be registered for use as documentation, for internal management and for making economic accounting after the crop production year.

Normative standards 
These includes crop standards, varieties, organic fertilisers, mineral fertilisers, plant protection products, and operations. Users can see other users’ normative standards in an anonymised form and copy these for own use, possibly after editing to make the specific standard relevant for the enterprise in question, for instance concerning the price. 

General
App settings includes:
•	Country affiliation of normative standards.
•	Area unit – ha, sqm., decar, etc.
•	Fertilisation strategy, either a maintenance or a sufficiency strategy
CropManager works with “admin users”, to which member businesses are registered. An admin user can register an unlimited number of enterprises, and invite an unlimited number of “normal” users for sharing access their data in CropManager. 

CropManager is prepared for users’ ability to import or manually register data from public registers, such as Integrated Administration and Control System (IACS) data, including data from the Land Parcel Identification System (LPIS) and the Herd and Animal Register, in order for CropManager to work on the basis of this information and be compliant with FaST (Farm Sustainability Tool) principles, and use the same data as basis as is used by the official control bodies.

Read more details in the attached Technical Documentation.

description.description:
MesParcelles is a solution for managing agricultural plots online, for a centralized, optimized and secure management of the farm:
    - Manage the traceability of your crops
    - Tracking your environmental indicators
   -  Facilitating your environmental certification audits
    - Managing your economic performance
   -  Anticipate your CAP declaration

MesParcelles allows you to make your CAP declaration simply on your own or with the help of an expert adviser.

MesParcelles allows you to calculate the doses of Nitrogen, Phosphorus and Potassium fertilizers according to the needs of your crops and your yield objectives. 

MesParcelles allows you to perform a number of regulatory checks on the forecast inputs and timing of application

MesParcelles provides access to a catalogue of phytosanitary products including all the products registered in France.
MesParcelles allows you to systematically check each of your phytosanitary entries:
- Of the registration of the phytosanitary product and the dose on the crop
- The compatibility of the mixture
- The respect of the re-entry deadline
- The respect of the pre-harvest period.

MesParcelles facilitates environmental certification audits (High Environmental Value, Global Gap).

MesParcelles allows you to connect your operating data with your tractor console to increase efficiency.

MesParcelles allows you to make the right decision at the right time by being connected with decision support tools

description.name: SQAPP Soil Quality Assessment
description.title: The Soil Quality Mobile App (SQAPP) offers an easy-to-use tool that brings global soil data into the decision-making sphere of land users and other interested end users.
description.description: The Soil Quality Mobile App (SQAPP) offers an easy-to-use tool that brings global soil data into the decision-making sphere of land users and other interested end users. SQAPP can, for any terrestrial location in the world for which there are soil data, provide the user with selected soil quality indicators and soil threat indicators. Rather than scoring soil quality in absolute terms, the app scores the soil quality relative to the average soil quality within areas with similar soil and climatic conditions (so-called pedo-climatic zones). For example, 80% means that only 20% of the area of the selected pedoclimatic zone is considered to have a higher indicator value than the point location being assessed. Soil threats are scored based on general, scientifically-derived threshold values. Both soil quality indicators and soil threat indicators with low scores are indicated and management solutions are suggested for the combination of indicators flagged as low in a specific location.

description.name: Monet Viticultura
description.title: Control of the vineyard state from any Internet connected device
description.description:
Monet Viticultura is a web platform to control the current state of the vineyard and its evolution from any Internet connected device

The tool incorporates algorithms for assessing and predicting the risk of disease in the vineyard (mildew, powdery mildew, botrytis and black rot) and the evolution of phenology. This information allows the viticulturist to anticipate the appearance of the disease and to plan tasks efficiently.

To run the disease and phenology prediction models we need to collect meteorological data from the vineyard, offering three possibilities:
1. Use weather stations that are already installed in the vineyard and adapt them, if possible, to transmit to the server managed by Monet.
2. Use public weather stations close to the vineyard that are representative of its behaviour.
3. Install new weather stations in locations of interest in the vineyard.

The platform also includes vegetation maps obtained through the processing of aerial images gathered from satellites, drones or aircrafts. This information allows the vineyard zoning and the classification of the grapes prior to harvest, information of great interest for effective production management.

Monet Viticultura is completed with a document management system that allows the user to enter information on the tasks carried out in the vineyard and any incident detected in it, integrating, in a single platform, all the information that winegrowers need to carry out their daily work.

The user will receive a daily report on the state of the vineyard by email and will also have a mobile APP available for iOS and Android.

description.name: AGRODATO
description.title: Engineering Technological Services for smart farming based on Sensor’s data, Remote Sensing and Analytics, all integrated in a single software for an optimized crop management.
description.description:
Agrodato is based on three pillars: Remote Sensing, Sensor´s data & targeted Analytics.

AGRICULTURAL REMOTE SENSING
By generation of vegetation indexes from satellite & high-resolution UAV images, we have information of photosynthetic, nutritional, hydric and health status of the crop, information that the naked eye is unable to see. This information is highly useful for crop assessments and improves decision-making.

MONITORING BY SENSORS
Implementation of weather stations, soil probes, dendrometers and real-time weighing devices, among others, to measure parameters of high agronomic value such as humidity, temperature, rainfall, wind, etc. The sensors network is located in pre-selected points following representativeness and variability criteria within the crops. Data is collected in the Software for real-time monitoring and representation.

DATA ANALYSIS & PROCESSING
Targeted and georeferenced sampling of soil, fruit, pests, leaf, etc. is made in the crop and extrapolated to the whole farm.
With the integrated processing of multisource data, we achieve a complete technical-scientific diagnosis of the global state of the crop.

AGRODATO´s workflow:
1.Data collection along the full crop productive cycle; study of photosynthetic activity using multispectral camera; chemical and biological analysis in the lab or through sensors on site.
2.Application of Artificial Intelligence for data processing. Algorithms development and cutting-edge data processing systems.
2.Technical advisory and action plans implementation through output interpretation.

Following this process AGRODATO offers integral service making information accessible to all professionals involved in agricultural production and responsible for optimization of agricultural holdings.

Developments are based on previous digitalization, zonation and thorough study of farms. Following works and reports are created:
Digital Field notebook (agricultural operations management)
Crop characterization: vegetative canopy, vigor (NDVI, LAI, ...)
Maps & graphics for easy data integration & visualization
Fertilization & irrigation plans (eco-sustainable)
Yield & crop quality estimations
Phytopathology alerts

This powerful tool operates in all type of crops: linear, discontinuous and extensive crops and highly improves crop management to farmers having the benefits of high technification, production increase, efficient use of resources, sustainability, adapted to customer and early cost effectiveness.

description.name: MARK-online
description.title: Nutrient Management tool for farmers and advisors
description.description:
Danish farmers` owned company SEGES developed a
nutrient management tool which is able to address current challenges in nutrient
management (Mark Online). Development, maintenance and refining of the tool has
been completely covered by software license fees without notable financial
government support. Because of the private ownership of the tool, there have been
no farmers’ concern that the data fed into Mark Online could be used for other
purposes than nutrient management planning.
Farm advisors were the key actors in spreading knowledge about the tool and
implementing it in practice. Every advisor received intensive trainings by the tool
developers and was supported by adequate information material such as detailed
manuals.
The use of the tool has became mandatory since the Denmark imposed
additional taxes on nitrogen fertilisers for farms which did not apply a sophisticated
nutrient management tool. Currently, about 2.6 million ha in Denmark is being
planned with Mark Online, either by the farmers themselves or by advisors on behalf
of the farmers.

description.name: Trinity Global Farm Pioneers
description.title: Trinity Global Farm Pioneers: Knowledge Exchange Platform Cultivating Tomorrow's Sustainable Agriculture Today
description.description:
In an era where agriculture sits at the crossroads of innovation and sustainability, Trinity Global Farm Pioneers emerges as a beacon for progressive farmers, visionary researchers, and dynamic industry stakeholders. Reaching out to every corner of the globe, this web and app platform is designed to revolutionise the way we view, practice, and understand farming.

Empowerment through Knowledge: Our primary aim is to arm every member with cutting-edge agricultural knowledge that not only enhances productivity but ensures it is achieved sustainably. Trinity Global Farm Pioneers pledges to bridge knowledge gaps and facilitate the dissemination of the latest scientific insights. 

Community Beyond Borders: Agriculture is not just about crops and livestock; it's about the people behind every harvest and every innovation. Trinity Global Farm Pioneers is a melting pot where farmers, experts, and enthusiasts from different backgrounds, cultures, and experiences converge. Here, ideas are exchanged, collaborations are realised, and a shared passion for sustainable agriculture grows.

Virtual Library of Innovations: Imagine a digital space where the latest agricultural research is just a click away. We've curated a vast repository of resources, studies, and articles to ensure that our community remains at the forefront of agricultural advancement.

Events and Networking: Beyond the virtual realm, Trinity Global Farm Pioneers connects its members to real-world events, conferences, and seminars. From hands-on workshops to insightful talks by industry leaders, members are always in the loop and never miss out on opportunities to expand their horizons.

The Pioneering Advantage: Being a part of Trinity Global Farm Pioneers is more than just membership—it's a commitment to the future. As we collectively strive to propel agriculture into the next era, every member becomes a catalyst for change, innovation, and sustainability.

Trinity Global Farm Pioneers pledges to uphold the tenets of sustainable farming, to continuously strive for innovation, and to foster a community that is both inclusive and visionary.

GET A HEAD START: Use group chat to meet and communicate before the workshop.

ACKNOWLEDGE DIFFERENCES: participants describe their environment to build team rapport.

description.name: PastureBase
description.title: PastureBase Ireland is the number one tool to help Irish dairy, beef, and sheep farmers manage their grass production and utilisation
description.description:
Since 2013, Ireland’s national agricultural research organisation Teagasc, have provided PastureBase Ireland (PBI) as a grassland management system to provide information and advice for Irish farmers. PBI is a grassland management decision support tool and a mechanism to capture background data on farms. PBI stores all grassland measurements in a common structure. It allows the quantification of grass growth and Dry Matter (DM) production (total and seasonal) across different enterprises, grassland management systems, regions, and soil types using a common measurement protocol and methodology. The capture, through the database, of on-farm grassland measurements from research and commercial farms will provide valuable strategic data for the grassland industry. 

PastureBase has 5,000+ users. Among other features, the PastureBase database has weekly records of grass covers for individual farms from 2013 to the present. A grass cover for a farm is principally, the amount of grass available on that farm for cows to eat; formally, it is a measure of biomass in grass on the farm above ground level or a height of 4cm. PBI allows farmers to enter this grass cover data for each field/paddock of their farm in a given week, using their own measurements/estimates, thus allowing them to budget grass-availability for their herd. Additional features are continuously being added to improve the user experience and the quality of the information available to the farmer user. 

Why farmers and advisors use PastureBase?
-PastureBase Ireland farmers grow more grass than the average farmer (NFS)
-Increased grass utilisation equates to more profit for the farmer
-Able to enter data through our smart phone app
-Benchmark against other farms in your discussion group or locality
-An array of tools, grass wedge, rotation planners, budgets, reports

How using PastureBase benefits the farmer and their farm?
-Can quantify the amount of grass on their farm on a given day
-Farmer is able to plan their grazing week ahead
-Easier to communicate to farm employees
-Able to calculate the amount of grass grown on the farm
-Increase output – livestock are grazing high quality grass throughout the grazing season
-Able to use supplementation strategy – reduce input costs

PastureBase is easy to use:
-A smartphone app is available
-The app works offline
-Export data to PDF and excel, so you can save or email results to others
-One app can manage multiple farms

Smart Farm Assistant is a modular system, which enables you to start with just a few modules and expand in a next phase with additional modules, when needed. The modular flexibility enables you to choose the solution best fitted to your business. Our systems can also be linked to existing systems (ERP or other) either collecting data from it or transferring data to it.

description.name: Hawk-EyeTM System
description.title: Hawk-Eye™ Systems, using thermal and visual image data, precisely monitor and measure and report crop health, stress, quality, and guide irrigation.
description.description:
Hawk-Eye™ Systems are fully autonomous, 24/7/365, remote sensing and reporting systems that image and measures canopy temperature in the far-IR band and canopy color in the visual band from image data captured and reported every ten minutes, day and night. From the data indices, notices, and enhanced imagery of the crop are available to certified clients in real-time via internet to phones, mobile devices, and desktops. Measurements are processed in real-time to assess and report canopy color, homogeneity, and crop stress; and to provide irrigation guidance. The indices can be formatted to actuate irrigation, fertigation, and precision applications of herbicides, pesticides and other amendments. All the imagery and data are archived for look backs and record keeping.
The Hawk-Eye™ camera set is easily established near the crop to gain a view of the canopy. Camera sets may be mounted on trees, poles, mast mounted trailers, buildings, or any vantage point that has a view of the crop. The associated mobile communications sub-set uses the local cell network to communicate the data from the field to Hawk-Eye™’s cloud. The field unit can be powered by DC or AC power and can be easily relocated when needed.
The Hawk-Eye™ System distills the extremely large amounts of data to provide pertinent and actionable real-time messages from the crop. Use it to scout for early warning of disease, pests, drainage issues, and the impact of cultivation practices.  Use it to prescribe irrigation. It will recommend needed irrigation and can be tuned to provide deficit irrigation guidance.
Measure and listen to what your crop has to say. Save time and make money. Grow quality.
Practice Sustainability.

description.name: VIPS
description.title: Reducing the environmental and health risks related to the use of pesticides in plant production
description.description:
The main objective is to reduce the environmental and health risks related to the use of pesticides in plant production. One of the main activities is to produce and distribute reliable forecasts of the risks of damages by pests on agricultural crops. The data system is designed to be both a decision support system for the producer and a tool for bringing information between the consumer, the producer, the extension service and the researcher.

The risk warning is based on local information about pest development, weather prognoses from Norwegian Meteorological Institute and weather data from Nibio's weatherstations (LMT), and models that calculate risk of damages from pests and diseases. Results are presented on www.vips-landbruk.no.

This new version of VIPS is an open source technology platform for implementation of IPM tools, aimed at international collaboration and local adaptations

description.name: Best4Soil
description.title: BEST4SOIL - A NETWORK OF PRACTIONERS, FOR SHARING KNOWLEDGE ON PREVENTION AND REDUCTION OF SOIL BORNE DISEASES
description.description:
With Best4Soil we are building a community of practice network across Europe by inter-connecting growers, advisers, educators and researchers. This network promotes knowledge ready for practice on 4 best practices (compost, green manure, anaerobic disinfestation, (bio) solarisation) for the control of soil borne diseases. Therefore we build a website and organize meetings and events in 20 European countries where we exchange knowledge on soil health with our communities of practice. The main objective of the Best4Soil thematic network is to maintain, improve or re-establish soil health in Europe. We provide open-access databases with information on the range of pathogens and nematodes that affect vegetable, arable and cover crops to help practitioners to build appropriate crop rotations and innovative control strategies. 
Innovative control strategies are provided through easily understandable tutorial videos and through factsheets which give more in-depth information. All information is edited in 22 EU languages, will be freely accessible and highly comprehensible to guarantee a smooth knowledge transfer from research to practice.

description.name: éPILOTE
description.title: Assist wheat producers in monitoring wheat plots during the season
description.description:
Exceptional weather events affecting crops have become more frequent and varied in recent years. The éPILOTE tool aims to help wheat producers optimize their nitrogen fertilization and irrigation.
This Decision Support tool is based on a crop model coupled with remote sensing data in order to follow the wheat state during the cropping season. With the web app, the farmer automatically receives an alert which advises him about the state of his fields and the necessity to perform a technical operation.

éPILOTE improves the water and nitrogen efficiency of the wheat crop. The average économic gain is 83 €/ha on the plots monitored over the two years of experimentation.

description.name: Farm Advisors on local radio
description.title: Advisors record, edit and submit daily farming and rural radio content to Mid West Radio local radio station to be published at a regular time each day.
description.description:
Twelve advisors on a 3 week rota, do a circa 4-5 minute pre-prepared monologue on 2-3 current topics each day, on Mondays, Tuesdays, Thursdays, and Fridays. These are broadcast after the 10 pm news on the local radio station Mid West Radio: MWR.
Six advisors also rotate to do a  circa 10-12 minute weekly interview slot with relevant Teagasc staff , farmers or people with something to offer, that is included as a regular insert on a Farming Matters program on Wednesday nights between 10.00 – 11.00 PM.
The rota is set up and manged locally, everyone knows when they’re on: a log is kept on the Teagasc internal document management system to avoid duplication of topics.
The broadcasts are done throughout the year apart from August, Christmas, and Public Holidays, so its 5 days a week most of the year.
There are  4 dedicated desk-top computers, microphones, and basic mixing desks in the 4 regional offices: recordings are made (monologue or interview) on relevant software: it used to be Cool Edit in the past; its Adobe Audition now. This allows us to edit what we do: trim it / bring up volume / add in extra bits etc.
With current Covid 19 restrictions offices are closed phone Apps are being used to do the slots at home: Clear Record, Auphonic, Voice Recorder are the free Apps of choice: downside is that you can’t edit the piece on the phone: in the offices we could record on the phone app, and e-mail it  and edit it on Adobe Audition on the dedicated desktop. Pieces can be e-mailed to MWR and they can do a bit of trimming pauses at the start and end of it if we ask them to. 
Going back a few years when it started some training was given by MWR personnel about how to use the technology, and how to do the programs in terms of delivery / speed / pace / number of topics etc. etc.: how to make it sound good / how to keep listeners engaged etc. New presenters have got some training since: not all of them; some advisors take to it  more easily than others, experienced advisors assist the newer presenters when they start. The co-operation with MWR has been good, we try to deliver MWR listeners require, and both ourselves and MWR want it to sound as professional as possible.

description.description:
VetIMPRESS is a powerful data management and exchange platform delivering impactful data at animal, farm, practice, and industry level via cloud-based apps.  VetIMPRESS was born out of a desire to strengthen the important role farm vets perform in providing advice around food production and protecting animal health, animal welfare, human health and the sustainability of the world’s resources by creating the records needed to inform decisions for vets and their breeders using standardised, permissioned, secure veterinary data.

The cloud-based platform and mobile app fits the unique practical needs of farm vets, allowing users to capture data and access insights from this data in real-time. VetIMPRESS’ core technology integrates information from a range of sources including government records, industry schemes, milk processors, laboratories, on-farm IT systems and farm service providers alongside vet-entered records, to provide the most complete picture of a cows health.

description.name: Trimble Ag Software: Advisor Prime
description.title: Trimble Ag Software: Advisor Prime Solution
description.description:
Advisor Prime is Trimble’s intelligent, fully integrated web-based solution featuring streamlined workflows for creating and sharing management zones and variable rate prescriptions. By using Advisor Prime, you’ll reduce data entry errors created by repetitive field-by-field procedures, cut out hours of processing time, and eliminate steps in transferring data back to the farmer.

Precision Zone Management — Advisor Prime’s robust zone management tool reduces the time and effort required to detect patterns in multiple data layers, including crop health imagery, EC data, elevation maps, or yield data. Use your own style and agronomic principles to:

• Select number of zones
• Set zone sizes
• Edit zones
You can then apply these settings to multiple clients, saving time and reducing headaches.

Mobile Soil Sampling — Advisor Prime comes with with the Trimble® Ag Mobile app, available for both Apple iOS and Android devices. When it comes to soil sampling, no other app makes the process easier. Create grids on the fly or display background zones for each field. See where you logged the actual point and send all data back to your computer without worrying about USB sticks or cables. With our mobile app you can:

• Create any grid size
• Load background zone maps
• Navigate to target points
• Complete work without an internet connection

Soil Lab Compatibility — Advisor Prime users can work with any soil lab and take advantage of our streamlined process to import soil test results for your clients. Further, you can easily print bag labels and lab submission sheets by field or in bulk. Advisor Prime makes it easy to manage the soil analysis process and create prescriptions from the resulting data.

Variable Rate Prescriptions — How much time do you spend creating fertility and seeding prescriptions and sharing them with farmers? This manual process can result in many painstaking hours and unexpected errors — right in the middle of your busiest time of year. With Advisor Prime, you can define when formulas are used and how fertilizer applications will be calculated, to make both initial prescriptions and last-minute changes quick and easy.

The best part? Your variable rate prescriptions can be easily shared with customers, especially if they use Farmer Pro. Once permission is granted, connect with your customers’ Farmer Pro accounts and quickly share prescriptions without a USB drive. Advisor Prime supports a wide range of controllers to drive field applications. No other software solution is easier to use.

Clean Yield Data — Are you tired of looking at yield maps that don’t match what’s in the field? Trimble Ag Software’s new Yield Data Cleaning tool automatically fixes issues with grain flow delays, GPS errors, sensor errors, and overlaps — saving you countless hours manually fixing them field-by-field. As more and more farmers move toward precision ag, accurate yield maps become critical when using them for prescriptions or management zones. Clean yield data means accurate maps, which result in more profitable acres.

Crop Scouting — Advisor Prime includes the ability to leverage the Trimble Ag Mobile app for crop scouting. The best part? Multiple users can enter crop scouting records at the same time, sending data back to one central location. Advisors can quickly analyze the results and create recommendations in a timely manner.

With our mobile app you can:

• Pick from pre-populated pest and weed lists
• Share real-time reports
• Capture photos and tag them to scouting job
• Complete work without an internet connection

description.name: Agroclimatic Information System for Irrigation (SiAR)
description.title: It allows the consultation and download of daily, weekly or monthly meteorological data from one or several stations.
description.description:
1- Description of the project
The Agroclimatic Information System for Irrigation (SiAR) is an infrastructure that captures, records and disseminates the agroclimatic data necessary for calculating the water demand of irrigation areas, which allows for obtaining useful, rigorous and quality information and contributes to better planning, management, handling and control of irrigation operations.

The Ministry, through the Subdirectorate General of Irrigation and Water Economy has been developing, maintaining and updating SiAR for more than 13 years with the aim of being an instrument to meet the technological demands of all irrigation professionals and to achieve these objectives, SiAR is based on these four fundamental pillars:

Reliability of agroclimatological data: Having reliable information and providing greater accuracy in determining it.
Advice to irrigators: Optimising irrigation doses by means of knowledge of the most representative meteorological data, thus responding to the technological demands of irrigators in view of the consolidation and improvement of irrigation.
Economic and environmental benefits: reducing operating costs, optimizing water use and improving energy efficiency.
Planning, administration and management of water resources. By strengthening the technical strategies of the water administration for the planning, administration and management of both ground and surface waters.
Obtaining reliable data, together with the application of the most appropriate formula for calculating reference evapotranspiration for each area, provides greater accuracy in determining the water needs of irrigated crops.

description.description: The Central Computer System for the Control of Veterinary Antibiotic Prescriptions (PRESVET) is an effective tool for monitoring and surveillance of antibiotic prescriptions made on livestock farms in order to adopt the measures required for their control. PRESVET centralizes the information on veterinary prescriptions for antibiotics, including part of the information that makes up the veterinary prescription, and which is essential to meet the objective of monitoring and controlling the use of antibiotics in food-producing animals.

description.name: Teagasc Podcasts - The Dairy Edge, Ovicast, The Beef Edge
description.title: Dairy, Sheep and Beef Podcast
description.description:
Teagasc launched 3 podcasts, one for dairy called 'The Dairy Edge', one for sheep called 'Ovicast' and one for beef called 'The Beef Edge'. Podcasts are free audio shows that anyone with an iPhone or Android phone can listen to.

The Dairy Edge is targeted at dairy farmers and people working in the dairy industry . Ovicast is targeted at sheep farmers and people working in the sheep industry. While The Beef Edge is targeted at beef farmers and people working in the beef industry. 

The series of podcasts aims to provide up-to-date advice and insights to Irish farms. You can go back and listen to any podcast you like, as often as you like. You can download it for free on to your smartphone and listen via your hands-free car stereo, the tractor cab or anywhere else that suits and is safe.

The Dairy Edge is Teagasc’s weekly dairy podcast for farmers. Presented by Emma-Louise Coffey the podcasts will cover the latest information, insights and opinion to improve your dairy farm performance.You can catch up on all the shows and interviews from the podcast on the Teagasc website at www.teagasc.ie/thedairyedge.

OviCast is the Teagasc Sheep Podcast which is presented by Ciaran Lynch. It brings you the latest advice, insights and technical updates for the sheep
industry. Find out more information here www.teagasc.ie/ovicastpodcast

The Beef Edge is Teagasc’s fortnightly beef podcast for farmers. Presented by Catherine Egan the podcasts will cover the latest news, information and advice to improve your beef farm performance.Find out more information here www.teagasc.ie/thebeefedge

You can listen to the podcasts on Apple and Google podcasts, as well as Spotify.

description.name: SaniTRAX
description.title: SaniTRAX, monitoring of health and antibiotics use on your farm
description.description:
SaniTRAX® is a new system for health monitoring, including medicines and vaccination control for pigs (nursery and grow-finish), with continuous on-line support from an expert veterinarian.

It is easy to use for farm workers, veterinarians and managers and allows knowing health status of each batch, farm or company, including the diseases present, their distribution over time, the treatments used and their dosage and  withdrawal periods with smart advice in his use. 

It can be managed real-time in any device (cell phones, tablets or PCs) and has unique features, most of them unique in the market. 

The concept of SaniTRAX® is completely new and largely disruptive, as it involves several innovations that are not available on the market until now. Among them are:

1.	Provides a carefully and accurate remote health control and monitoring from farms worldwide
2.	Promotes a judicious use of antibiotics, ensuring proper dosing and warnings before a wrong administration or not respecting withdrawal period
3.	Keeps evidences of health plans and working protocols for quality assurance and certification schemes.
4.	Facilitates professional advice from expert veterinarians in real-time.
5.	Generates warnings and alerts to avoid later problems.
6.	Creates a farm multimedia library storing valuable material.
7.	Provide easy and fluent communication with farm staff and managers. 
8.	Aggregates and consolidate farms information for a broader understanding of health and production in the whole company.
9.	Allows fast and accurate field trials.

description.name: VanBoven Farm Advisory Service
description.title: VanBoven - Start offering your clients drone-mapping services with the click of a button.
description.description:
In order to remain on top of the competition, farm advisors are always on the lookout for new opportunities for their clients. Technology can offer such opportunities. However, adopting and using new technologies often requires significant time investments from the advisors.

One such technology is the use of drones for agricultural mapping and monitoring purposes. Most advisors agree that accurate and up-to-date field maps would be beneficial for their clients, increase the efficiency of in-field inspections and help in communication with the client. However, adopting this technology is burdensome and requires investments in training and IT infrastructure.

VanBoven offers a service for advisors that tackles exactly this issue. The service is based around a proprietary software platform that helps in four areas:

1. Advisors should not have to fumble and experiment with camera and drone settings, flight paths etc. Therefore, with VanBoven, the advisor simply indicates what field he or she would like to map. The platform then generates the optimal flight path and settings and sends this to the drone over the air. The advisor only has to turn on the drone and press 'Go' in a mobile app. The whole flight is then executed autonomously.

2. A drone gathers up to 2.000 images of a single field. Advisors should not have to plough through this pile of data on their computers. Therefore VanBoven provides a small hardware device that can be used to automatically upload all images to the platform - no computers required! The platform automatically sorts out all the photos and stores them safely.

3. Once uploaded to the platform the images are automatically combined into a single field map. The result is a map similar to those in Google Earth, but with a resolution of up to 5 millimeters. Optionally index maps (Leaf Area Index, Green Area Index etc) can be derived.

4. The results are presented within 24 hours in a secure online environment. Advisors can log-in and inspect the captured fields, create annotations and/or share the maps with the client. This online environment can be provided as a white-label service, such that it fully integrates with the advisor's existing web-services and branding.

With VanBoven's service, farm advisors can offer drone services to their clients with minimal investment in training and technology.

description.name: MePAR
description.title: Agricultural Parcel Identification System (MePAR)
description.description:
The Agricultural Parcel Identification System (MePAR) is the exclusive national identification system for land support procedures under Article 67 (2) of Regulation (EU) No 1306/2013. Exclusive in the sense that only data from this identification system can be used when applying for EU funding, in whole or in part, related to land. When applying for such subsidies, the data of any other register (eg the land register) cannot be taken into account against the data of the MePAR, be it the location, identification number or even the area of the agricultural plots. 
Basically, this system is needed because the legislation of the European Union and Hungary stipulates that the subsidies in question are
mandatory. From the 2004 grant year onwards, the MePAR will ensure the clear identification of the location of the agricultural parcels on which 
land are based exactly where and will help to provide a simple and accurate indication of their area. In a GIS system, with the map-based aerial view background, the data required for the application and supporting it are available. 
The MePAR is used by the farms themselves at the time of application and by the agency during the application management and control.

description.name: CypruSaves
description.title: A tool for Water and Field Management and Water and Carbon Footprint Estimation
description.description:
CypruSaves is a web GIS based platform that offers services to farmers and agricultural processing companies. For the moment provides service for the wine sector of Cyprus. Is a tailored made tool, easily conformed to other crop types and locations.
Its specificity is based on the use of specific crop growing parameters, field soil properties and local climatic data, which in turn provide consultancy of higher reliability and accuracy. Raw data used for the consultancy is downstreamed at vine variety level.
For the moment, the CypruSaves platform offers the following services for 14 vine varieties growing in Cyprus:
1. Estimation of the Water Footprint of the cultivation and of the processing plant. All three components of the Water Footprint, i.e., Blue, Green, and Grey, are calculated.
2. Definition of irrigation needs
3. Definition of parasitic diseases outbreak in relation to climatic conditions
4. Fertilization recommendations based on crop needs and soil properties
5. Calculation of the carbon footprint and the emission of Greenhouse gases (GHG) of the cultivation and the processing unit (wineries).
6. Evaluation of the suitability of an area for the establishment of cultivation (currently vine) according to the climate and soil properties.

description.name: PeHeStat
description.title: Performance and Health Statistics based on thorough analysis of animal health, poultry house and laboratory results leading to individual advice to poultry farmers.
description.description:
PeHeStat stands for Performance and Health Statistics. Our main objective can be deducted from this. Health parameters, house parameters and laboratory results from the industrial poultry sector are collected and analysed. Based on a thorough analysis, PeHeStat can then give individual advice to the poultry farmers.
In order to collect and analyse zootechnical data from farms, we use 2 tools: Porphyrio’s Smart Farm Assistant and Poultrack, our own developed software. PeHeStat distributes the software and takes care of the technical follow-up. Based on the data collected and analysed within Poultrack and Smart Farm Assistant, PeHeStat can provide individual advice to the poultry farmer. The result: a better production process, a healthy poultry herd and a safe end product.
Poultrack® was developed in-house and can be summarised as an online portal where all documents that belong to the mandatory company register are classified. These include administration-and-delivery-documents, contracts, feed coupons, laboratory results, slaughter reports … . The portal provides an administrative simplification for poultry veterinarians. With just a few clicks, you can retrieve that one document you are looking for. As a poultry farmer, you cannot acces the Poultrack® portal, but there is a direct link between this file management and the file manager within the Smart Farm Assistant of Porphyrio®
The Smart Farm Assistant is an online tool specifically for the poultry sector. It enables managers to closely monitor their animals and prevent imminent problems. Farm data such as feed and water intake, temperature, etc. can be imported into the Smart Farm Assistant through a link with the farm computer. When working with production cards, they can be imported as well. Because all data are collected online and remain available at all times, a farm manager can easily compare parameters within a flock or between the current flock and historical flocks. In this way, it helps to make strategic and well-considered decisions.

description.name: Teagasc Crop report platform
description.title: Seasonal analysis and updates of technical, agronomic information  relevant to crop growers their advisors and suppliers
description.description:
The Crop Report platform is an extensive source of up to date information prepared by Teagasc Specialists, it includes field observations, the latest scientific trial results and practical management strategies for all the major crops in Ireland throughout the growing season.  
It is regarded as the industry standard for technical agronomy strategies.  The Crop Report has continually developed the information and format every year since publication, and a recent redesign is particularly suitable as a digital format. The Crop Report has a dedicated following of commercial agronomists and industry professionals subscribing every year and is regarded as a critical part of the technical Knowledge Transfer to the tillage industry.  
The Crop Report now can be viewed by anyone through a desktop web app and on mobile app platforms (Apple and Android).  This allows the user to save their preferences across platforms so that information of most interest to them can be viewed, either in the office, or out in the field on the mobile app.  All new users can avail of a 14 day free trial when first logged in.   
Agronomist use the Crop Report throughout the year.   Crop Reports are used regularly when walking crops, as it not only has the most up to date information on the current status of crops and a scientific rationale for specific management decisions.
The Crop Report resonates with farmers as the technical information is presented alongside detailed product information on fertilisers, plant protection products and agronomy strategies.  Regular updates on crops and issues in the field give farmers an early warning of possible issues to watch out for locally.  The Crop Report is available to Teagasc Clients who avail of a technical service from Teagasc.  However all farmers can partake in the 14 day free trial.  
The Crop Report systematically collates the available plant protection products which are commonly used by farmers each year. There are a wide array of herbicides, fungicides, etc. which are similar, but some may be more suitable in specific situations.  Products tables contain commentary about getting the best from these products in a safe and environmentally sustainable way. It is easy to quickly navigate to this information in the Crop Report app”.    
The Crop Report includes not only tillage crop information but a range of information around grassland, environment and cross compliance issues.

description.name: Teagasc Short messaging System (SMS) for messaging client farmers and students
description.title: A SMS system for advisors to keep in touch with clients through mobile/smart phone
description.description:
Teagasc Advisory and education services staff have used use a Short Messaging Service (SMS) to send regular targeted text messages to all its clients and students for the last 15 years. With more than 98% of client farmers using mobile phones this technology is fast, cheap, visual, mobile and programmable. Over the last 15 years the usage has grown from 15,000 text messages to over 2 million texts in 2019 to 45,000 clients and 8,000 students.Fast -	Text messages will arrive within minutes of being sent.  Group texts can be sent to multiple members at the same time.
Cheap -	At 4-5c/message these are considerably cheaper than post or voice message.
Visual -	We know that most people learn or remember better from a visual image than from a sound, e.g. seeing 4.00 p.m. Thursday written down will stick in the memory better than hearing it spoken.
Mobile -	For the message sender you can send individual and group texts from anywhere with phone reception. For the receiver, the message will remain on the phone until they decide to delete it.
Programmable - You can when sending texts, programme a set time and date in advance.  

The system is called PUCA www.puca.com and it is relatively old at 15years, it has been very popular with Teagasc staff particularly busy advisers who want to keep regular contact with their clients. This was the preferred system many years ago, it enabled multiple regional, sectoral, and advisor based groupings with the potential to send out SMS messages to as many or as few as required.

Looking at usage, the most common activity is the use of PUCA SMS to remind farmer clients of group meetings, local farm walks and regional/national events. It is also used extensively to remind farmers of critical dates. It has also been useful in giving alerts to farmers relating to weather and disease pressure.   

Advisors have in many cases progressed to using other messaging platforms Whats-app and various social media platforms. These offer a more interactivity and will no doubt continue to expand. Teagasc plan to link SMS to its new CRM system soon. 
During the first few months of this year with Covid 19 restrictions affecting advisory and education activities the SMS system has seen a rapid increase in use as advisors use it to alert farmer clients and students to the challenges faced and the workaround solutions in place.

description.name: AGROasesor
description.title: AGROasesor include different DATS to provide professional advice in different topics: irrigation, fertilization, varietal, diseases and sustainability KPIs
description.description:
AGROasesor has created advanced GIS tools for personalized advice for the sustainable management of extensive and horticultural crops. The platform helps farmers, farm managers and advisory services to achieve a more efficient and sustainable use of crops by making available all the technical knowledge available through innovative webGIS platform. AGROasesor lets to access to recommendations and specific advice at agricultural plot level (irrigation, fertilization, varietal, diseases and sustainability KPIs). Among these utilities, this platform provides field notebook, traceability and administrative reporting, crop phenology modelling at the parcel level, based in meteo data and a geolocalization of farms through a GIS viewer.  
The AGROasesor platform integrates field cultivation operations, with advice on crop management through the use of decision support tools (DST).
Each user incorporates the complete technical itineraries carried out in each plot, and can download the exploitation notebooks, updated with the SIGPAC for each campaign. They can access digital information from soil maps or satellite images, which are incorporated in their crop monitoring. Decision Support Tools (DST) allow the integration of existing knowledge with each crop’s itinerary in each plot, with soil management characteristics, and specific climatic conditions of the campaign.

It takes into account the weather, the stage of the crop, the risk of disease outbreaks and identifies the best times to fertilize, weed and protect the crop. It is available on all types of media: tablets, computers and smartphones.

appack is a system, where you can choose from different modules to assemble your own app that can be customized to your personal requirements. Especially in communication between advisors, farmers and each other in different ways, like chat or even video-conferencing is possible. Especially during situations, where no face-to-face advisory is possible actual, the video-conference-tool is useful to keep the connection to each other! 
Of course, there are several other actions possible!

description.name: CowsAndMore
description.title: Digital collection of animal behaviour data by advisor directly within the barn
description.description:
The aim of the project „CowsAndMore “was the development of an assistance system with which it is possible, using animal behavior criteria in free stall dairy barns. The digital root-cause analysis is based on a comparison of the individual farm with defined goals and comparison values of specific dataset. Moreover, scientific module calculations concerning animals, husbandry and management are used.

Through the digital collection of data with a tablet, technical production advice in the dairy industry will be further optimized. The objective and systematic collection of criteria and indicators in relation to behavior, disposition and metabolism if dairy cows, standardized root-causes analysis will identify weaknesses. The software will allow the further development of important approaches to optimization.

 

Approach to analysis of weak points

        Objective and systematic detection of animal-related parameters, the stable construction and management
        Data are analyzed using the evaluation software locally and graphed comparison with the reference values.
        Detection of weak points and their attitude related causes
        Concrete recommendations for improvement of housing conditionsRecording As a part of the evaluation, undesired deviations are marked as weaknesses I the evaluation graphs by the user. The program selects the weak point corresponding to the selected specific causes.Reasons and measuresThe user can then match measures to the selected causes

Users are several advisory services in Germany and Austria, among others also Austrian chambers of Agriculture.

benefits: Better interaction between farmer and advisor

description.name: e.GEBA
description.title: e.GEBA
description.description: After an app is downloaded and a farmer registers as a user, he/she can transfer data to his/her adviser about what fertilizers or plant protection products he/she used. Detailed information can be provided as a farmer can specify what amount of what fertilizer or plant protection product for what type of crop in what area and when was used. Documents and images can be uploaded as well. Every time a farmer enters data into the app, an adviser receives a notification about data submitted. The same, a farmer is sent a message when an adviser performs certain actions using data uploaded by a farmer.

benefits: Better interaction between farmer and advisor

description.name: Grassman
description.title: Mobile application dedicated to driving meadows from sowing to harvest
description.description:
The application includes six modules dedicated to driving the meadow:

- "Compose": Depending on the geographic area and the typology of the prairie soil, the application will suggest species combinations, sowing doses and types of varieties to the breeder.

- "Fertilize": Once the composition has been chosen, the farmer will be advised on the doses of fertilizers to be supplied and their distribution.

- "Identify": The farmer can enter the weeds present for each plot and estimate the degree of soiling.

- "Fight": After identifying the weeds, the application gives some advice to fight against them mechanically or chemically.

- “Mowing”: Thanks to the seven-day weather data for each plot entered in the software, Grassman identifies the weather windows suitable for the forage harvest by taking into account the dominant species in each meadow and the harvesting method.

- "Qualify": It will be possible to estimate the quality of the forage harvested by sensory criteria (odor, presence of mold, dust, fiber level, color ...). This will identify the quality for each plot and define the category of animals per batch harvested.

According to the founding companies, the tool is not primarily intended to market seeds or advice. It is an expertise tool. "Grassman must help the farmer make the right decisions that will allow him to find better profitability in the management of his forage system," explains Laurent Victor, marketing and communications director Jouffray-Drillaud.

The decision support tool will be offered to breeders for an annual subscription of € 50. Each user can, if they wish, share their reports and exchange with other breeders or with their adviser.

description.name: Watch it grow
description.title: Belgian information platform for digital follow up of field/crop
description.description: The online tool offers the opportunity to follow the development of crops. Furthermore, it allows to avoid diseases, decrease productivity and quality losses and discover which fields need action from the farmers' side. This allow to decrease costs and gain time while increasing yields. On a timely basis, farmers receive a detailed overview and messages about the status of the crops, allowing timely reaction of the farmer to improve yields in a sustainable way.

description.name: Smartphone App with Augmented Reality. Virtual visits around nature trails.
description.title: Tourist information app of the Nature Trails Network with augmented reality for iOS and Android devices
description.description:
The app lets you do self-guided tours of the Nature Trails which are distributed all over the country. Each trail has a detailed description, practical information and a route map.

Additionally, the augmented reality tool included in this app (available for many of the Nature Trails in the Network) lets the user explore the trail for themselves by following virtual signage and locating the main points of interest with virtual markers geolocated in the landscape when viewed through the mobile device.

These virtual markers are divided by topics, and contain information of interest about the point where they are situated, giving this app an enormous interpretive, informative and tourist potential.

benefits: Enhanced adaptation & resilience to climate change

description.name: Agrodox
description.title: AGRODOX- Simple farm managing app
description.description:
The Agrodox app enables every farmer to digitize his farm in a simple and free way, by allowing him to manage all the essential activities of his farm, monitor his financial situation, use a set of tools that will make it easier for him to make various calculations such as unit conversion, layout and number of seedlings, and it allows him to enter and track agro meteorological forecast for multiple locations,all user fields and various other useful and interesting features - all very simple, easy to understand and emphasize for free.

The application is adapted for easy use and data entry. The user can easily add his fields and enter for them activities such as tillage, fertilization, sowing, harvesting, etc., thus keeping records of work in one place, enabling him to enter the financial aspect of these activities and finally can easily get a report of all the work done in one click. In addition, the application will offer information from the world of agriculture and the ability to solve interactive educational quizzes that can improve agricultural practice.

description.name: AgriSnap
