description.description: The AgriSnap App allows advisors and farmers to take photographs of features and events in their fields and farms. This App reduces to need for on farm inspections and increases the accuracy of compliance for both advisors and farmers involved in environmental and other funded programmes. The App increases the digital mobility of advisors as they use phones and other devices to take quality images on farms. The App is downloaded from the Apple i store and enabled through the DAFM secure platform.

description.name: Animal Welfare scan - Dierenwelzijnsscan
description.title: Determine the level of animal welfare on the company
description.description:
There is a self-scan per animal species (cattle, pigs, poultry) and per industry (for cattle: dairy cattle and beef cattle).

Good animal welfare is achieved by complying with the 'five freedoms'. The welfare app contains indicators for each of these freedoms:

Free from hunger & thirst
Free of inconvenience
Free from pain, injury & illness
Free to show normal behavior
Free from anxiety and chronic stress.

The first part of the scan (tile GENERAL) can be completed by the cattle farmer at his desk. This involves questions about housing, breed and various key figures.

In addition, there are the scans for the barn itself. 

After completing the questionnaire, the farmer will automatically receive a report by e-mail. This also contains the welfare radar (see figure). It shows various welfare indicators on a scale from 0% (very poor) to 100% (very good). The farmer also receives more information online about possible risk factors (www.dierenwelzijnscan.be). He can then discuss with the farm veterinarian which measures may be applied to improve animal welfare.

If the scan is taken at regular intervals (e.g. twice a year), the evolution of the animal welfare level on the farm can also be monitored. The effect of management changes on animal welfare can also be investigated.

In addition, if there is sufficient participation, members of The Farmers Union of Flanders can compare their scores with those of fellow farmers. Of course, this is completely anonymous.

😏 Connect science and experience in a massive way 🚀 to accelerate the agroecological transition 😉

We try to contribute 😏 to this essential momentum 🌡 🔥 🌩 🌪 ❄ 🌊

description.name: PlantCare 3.0
description.title: phytosanitary app
description.description:
PlantCare is a very useful application (APP) for smartphones and tablets created by the technology company XG Innova. The application is aimed at agronomists, farmers, technicians, students and agricultural enthusiasts.

PlantCare is an excellent tool that makes it possible to find the right product and treatment to treat a crop from a specific pest. In addition, it is capable of providing lists of pests that can affect a crop and lists of crops that can be affected by a pest.

The data source of the APP is the one that appears in the Ministry of Agriculture, Food and Environment of Spain, which confers integrity and reliability of the data contained. Likewise, PlantCare makes it possible to directly view the technical record of the record without the need for mobile coverage or, if you prefer, you can share it on social networks, email, etc.

Thanks to its powerful search engine, you can easily find the phytosanitary product that combats the crop problem by multiple criteria: manufacturers, active ingredient, pests, cultivation and areas. Separately and within the Organic menu of the App, we will find the products that are considered suitable for organic crops, facilitating the information search needs of technicians and users.

The data is updated and verified periodically.

benefits: Better interaction between farmer and advisor

description.name: R.Campo
description.title: The app to collect data in the field, even when working offline
description.description:
R.Campo is an app developed by Ruralbit that allows you to record data in the field and later synchronize it with various platforms.
The use in the field is done completely offline. The collected data are, in the end, synchronized with the application (Genpro, e-Exploração, Ia-Açores or another application through backoffice), from which it is possible to obtain personalized reports.
R.Campo synchronizes directly with Ruralbit's online applications (e-Exploração, Genpro and IA-Açores). It is also possible to integrate R.Campo directly with other platforms, through webservice, thus increasing its potential use, in a simple way for the user. In these cases, through a simple backoffice, with a web interface, the user can parameterize the forms that will be needed in the field. After that, it is enough to synchronize with a mobile device and have the form ready to use.

O R.Campo sincroniza directamente com as aplicações online da Ruralbit (e-Exploração, Genpro e IA-Açores). Foi também criada a possibilidade de integrar o R.Campo directamente com outras plataformas, através de webservice, aumentando assim a sua potencial utilização, de uma forma simples para o utilizador. Nestes casos, através de um backoffice simples, com interface web, pode parametrizar os formulários que vai necessitar no campo. Depois basta fazer a sincronização com o equipamento móvel e tem o seu formulário pronto a utilizar.

The tasks can range from simple data collection operations to more complex operations that imply the existence of a plan, with predicted animals and, consequently, the possibility of checking in the field which animals are registered and which are missing.

The application will be available for several systems in order to be able to use it on your equipment of choice. The Android version (tablet or mobile phone) is currently available.
Synchronization is done directly, there is no need to migrate files: just have a network on the mobile device (GPRS or Wi-Fi) and click on synchronize; the system will do the rest on its own.

R.Campo is yet another service that Ruralbit offers in the form of Software-as-a-Service: the operator only pays for its use and for as long as it wants, which allows it to offer a very low usage price

description.description:
The P & K calculator is an online calculator for estimating P and K fertilizer requirements for grassland and crops. It is available online, or via the iOS and Android Smart Phone Apps. It requires details of crop yield, grassland stocking rate, concentrate feed usage, soil test results, and slurry application to estimate chemical fertilizer P and K requirements. The calculator is useful for advisors and farmers who want to the the optimum response from their use of organic and chemical fertilisers without exceeding their recommended usage levels in the growing season.

All calculations are consistent with Best Practice Recommendations from Teagasc.

AppsforAgri BV, in collaboration with OCI Agro, has developed the extensive Agro Weather App especially for farmers. Depending on your planned agro-activity, this app indicates the optimal time to get started.

description.description: Recognise nature in one click! Take a photo in Europe or the Dutch Caribbean, and find out what species it is. Collect all your nature observations in the app. You will be rewarded with tailored tips to find species, achieve badges and be invited to participate in challenges. Create groups with colleagues, family or friends and see each other's best nature observations. Your observations, and those of others, contribute to species research and knowledge of nature. Every observation counts!

description.name: Teagasc Fertiliser Limits Tracker Smart Phone App
description.title: Teagasc Mobile app to help farmers monitor fertiliser purchases with their recommendations or limits as calculated in their annual fertiliser plan.
description.description:
This Smart Phone App is provided by Teagasc advisors to farmers to help them track use of Nitrogen, Phosphorus and Potassium on farms. Advisors prepare annual fertiliser plans use the Online NMP system. The output of these fertiliser plans give a farmer a recommended NPK total Kgs usage figure.
Farmers enter the total NPK limit and add the purchases or usage as it goes. They can see the cumulative figures and assess their remaining allocation. it has a huge advantage in being mobile and available to the farmer 24/7 and at the point of decision making.
This app is a bit out of date and has been superseded, however it still gives the farmer some useful functionality. 
The app was developed jointly with Vodafone

description.name: Smartpharm
description.title: This application digitizes the veterinary register in breeding, to promote the traceability of treatments, to the strip / room / hut or to the animal, from birth to slaughter.
description.description:
This solution automates and simplifies the recording of information relating to health treatments by recording the dose and the animal treated. SmartPharm therefore constitutes a less tedious solution, avoiding manual entry in the breeding register and allows the exploitation of the information collected in a database. Thus, alerts are sent directly to the farmer's smartphone, for example in the event of too short a waiting period or an inappropriate dosage. The farmer can also compare his practices to IFIP references or even value animals that have received no treatment in a differentiated manner.

In practice, the farmer scans a contactless (NFC) "medication" card with his smartphone and assigns treatment to the animal using an RFID chip or to the room / box using an NFC card. A breeding-specific database gathers this information and the breeder can compare his practices with IFIP references on a Dashboard.

description.title: The vaccination app supports the vet in following a vaccination schedule so that animals are optimally protected.
description.description:
The vaccination app supports the vet in following a vaccination schedule so that animals are optimally protected.

The app offers a complete overview of the vaccinations administered and the appointments. Registrations are easy and with the push notifications you will never forget to make an appointment. That app also provides information about the products with associated product schedules. AppsforAgri BV has developed the Vaccination app in collaboration with Boehringer Ingelheim.

Push Notifications
Simple registrations
Available in: Europe, Middle East and Africa

description.description: BodenDok (SoilDOC) is a mobile application to monitor your soil with the spade diagnosis. It asks questions about the soil, which can be answered with a simple click. Questions related to your location, the crops grown, any problems occurring on the plot, and appearance of the soil surface. You are then instructed through the steps of the spade diagnosis and to answer related questions. Additional information and pictures help to find the answers. During the evaluation, the app collects all observations and generates a report, which can be exported as csv, txt, or html format, and can be saved as a PDF file on a computer. The app facilitates the comparison of different surveys at the same location. The app is available for Android and Apple devices.

description.name: HydroPlus - Smart Remote Management Irrigation System
description.title: HydroPlus
description.description: The Hydroplus system is a smart irrigation management controller system for professional farms and industry. It interacts with local hydraulic, agronomical and meteorological values adjusting automatically the timing and frequency og irrigation. The system has a modular design to suit different types and sizes of plots. The integration of different control and management equipment makes this tools of the most sophisticated in the market. Hydroplus stands out for its range of low-power, long range sensory communications networks and powerful software algorithms for auto-reset "water Balance".

description.name: NMA agro mobile app
description.title: "NMA agro" - a tool for farmers to notify NPA about agricultural activities and investments made.
description.description: The NMA agro mobile app has a dual purpose. It is intended for farmers who wish to inform the Agency about  the activities carried out on the fields, the investment projects they have implemented and the issues related to the commitments of the granted subsidies. In addition, using the mobile app, Lithuanian citizens can provide information about potentially abandoned fields. "NMA agro" transmits photos with precise coordinates (geotagged) and azimuth directly from the field, allows on-screen measurements of the distances and areas. The app includes Copernicus Sentinel satellite images, maps of different vegetation indexes, aerial imagery, boundaries of declared fields, helping farmers to monitor and evaluate the condition of crops grown on their fields and to perform necessary farm activities when needed. Satellite imagery and vegetation index maps are updated constantly during the main farming season. Mobile app is one of the component of an automated continuous field monitoring information system based on Sentinel satellite imagery. "NMA agro" is available free of charge at "Google Play" and "App store".

description.name: Teagasc Thinning Ready Reckoner
description.title: Thinning Ready Reckoner for Conifer Forests
description.description: The thinning ready reckoner is a useful indicator to assess if a spruce forest is ready for thinning. It should be used by forest owners in conjunction with professional forestry advice to assist with a thinning decision in all cases. It involves evaluating the representative stocking density per hectare and the average diameter at breast height (DBH) using sample plots within a forest. Once these parameters are known,  the Ready Reckoner can be used provide an indication if the forest may be ready for thinning. Note: other important decision criteria should also be assessed such location, exposure, drainage status etc.

description.description: The Teagasc Pig Department regularly create infographics on a range of topics to best best communicate valuable information to the pig industry in a user friendly format. description.name: Teagasc Johne's Disease: A Control Strategy
description.title: Johne's Disease: A Control Strategy
description.description: Sociological factors (i.e., farmers’ routines, practices, beliefs and values) regarding animal health have implications for disease management, specifically implementation of critical control points (CCPs, following HACCP) to control Johne’s disease at farm level. The research findings were channelled into a transdisciplinary co-design process, involving the interrogation of the findings by groups of farmers, veterinary practitioners, veterinary scientists, policy-makers, farm advisers, and cooperative representatives. In a sequence of workshops, the multi-actor groups co-designed a suite of practical actions to implement the CCPs at farm level. The process evolved to include an artist and graphic designer, who worked with the multi-actor team to translate the practical implementation actions into interactive and visual tools. In addition to recording templates (e.g., hygiene, calving etc., necessary for implementing the HACCP approach), information briefs and storyboards (illustrated stories) were co-designed. Based directly on farmers’ and other actors’ stories, storyboards have diverse purposes. They present ‘how to’ information graphically, while also incorporating in vernacular language the social (relationship), cultural (pride) and economic (monetary) factors that arise in decision making. Veterinarians and advisers can use storyboards to prompt discussion with farmers of contentious or sensitive issues, deflecting the focus onto a hypothetical case rather than focusing on the farmers personally. Storyboards were developed to show the negotiation of particular beliefs/values held by farmers in relation to disease control, necessary for practical implementation of the CCPs. Together with information briefs and CCP recording templates, the storyboards are presented in a comprehensive printed document, and in an interactive PDF for use on touchscreen and conventional devices such as tablets, androids and computers.

benefits: Better interaction between farmer and advisor

description.name: SMART milking - SMART melken
description.title: Tackling Nitrogen and Methane for Cattle Farming with a Future
description.description:
Dairy farming is also working on sustainability. Certainly in the emissions of both methane and ammonia, there is still considerable profit to be made.
The tools helps for determining a strategy based on different rations and additives to reduce the environmental impact.  Of course there is an eye for the economic picture: extra costs and influence on milk production are taken into account.  With this handy tool you can quickly find a strategy that suits your company. 

How does it work? 

- Move the bar per parameter (0 = not important at all / 10 = important at all) : CH4, Carbon Footprint, Milk Production, Nitrogen efficiency, Cost of the feed
- Select the desired strategy 
- Select the "Explanation" tab (top) for additional info on the parameters 
- Select the "Overview" tab (top) to view the result 
- Your most appropriate strategy is always at the top! 

There are information sheets on the climate effects of every ration and additive in the tool.

description.name: Best bull selection for insemination - Insemineer met het juiste rietje
description.title: The dairy tool ‘Best bull selection for insemination' aims to support dairy farmers in making their on-farm insemination strategy.
description.description:
The tool ‘Best bull selection for insemination' was created by Boerenbond and ILVO. This was done in consultation with a group of dairy farmers united in an Operational Group supported by the European Agricultural Fund for Rural Development. The aim of the tool is to help dairy farmers decide which insemination strategy to use on their farms.
Through the tool, it shows in a simple way what the additional yield can be if a dairy farmer uses sexed semen and use crosses.
After the farmer enters the specific figures for his farm in the farm variables tab, he can enter the sales prices he is paid in the market parameters tab. The purchase prices of the different types of semen are also set here.
Based on these parameters, a suggested optimum is displayed. First, the required number of calving heifers and the number of heifer calves to be kept for rearing are displayed.
Below that, the farmer finds three predefined scenarios with different shares of sexed heifers, sexed cows and crossbreds. He can adjust the percentages himself by playing with the sliders within each scenario.
Below the different scenarios, the results the farmer will achieve in each composition are visually displayed. By playing with the sliders, the farmer can thus see which combination gives him the best balance and can easily compare different combinations.
keywords: Insemination strategy - sexed semen - utility crosses - right semen - farm variables - market parameters - suggested optimum

description.name: Teagasc Bord Bia Carbon Navigator
description.title: This tool is a web service which allows farmers carbon impact to be measured relative to other farms based on practices and breeding merit of the herd.
description.description: Data collected from farms is entered into an on line web page and complimented with data from other on line services related to that herd. The data relates the breeding index of the herd (EBI) and practices such as the length of the grazing season, the age at first calving, nitrogen fertilizer and slurry use and timing. The tool helps advisors and farmers target areas on farms that can be improved. The Navigator compares individual performance with averages. There are 2 main version Dairy Carbon Navigator and Beef Carbon Navigator.

description.title: Interactive tool for pig farmers to select the most suitable boar lines for their farm.
description.description:
The testing of boar lines for the Flemish pig market is an instrument made for Flemish pig farmers with the aim of exposing the genetic differences in boar lines of breeding organizations. In this way, pig farmers can select the most suitable genetics for their farms. It is extremely important for the pig farmer to be able to use the best genetics to
to produce the desired end product as cost-effectively as possible. This choice affects the
technical and economic results of the company. In order to determine these offspring performances in an independent manner
boar lines crossed with hybrid sows on field farms and removed from the piglets
born from this crossbreed collected data on their performance. This data is collected at
made available to sow and pork farmers through an interactive tool.

description.name: EkonMOD milk
description.title: This application assists to better understand the dynamics of the dairy herd structure and to improve decision-making in Slovak conditions, including the GHG mitigation agenda
description.description: Generally, each of the application under the umbrella of the EkonMOD milk platform is used to evaluate the economic consequences of different on-farm strategies, including a farm-focused calculator for greenhouse gases (GHG) emissions from a user-specified dairy farm allow individual dairy farm managers to calculate the emission factors and yields of methane, ammonia and nitrous oxide of dairy cows, heifers, calves and fattened bulls, if present on dairy farm. The interactive dairy farm model approach was developed at farm level and based on a static approach. This modeling framework was built to serve the purposes of a wider research strategy. The main objective of this activities concerns an analysis of possible effects of changing conditions on different Slovak dairy cow operations. The model developed can be used to run various scenarios in the field of structural and technical change on dairy farms. Moreover, it can be tailored for dairy farms that differ in intensity and scale. The complex application analyses the input parameters of the breeding intensity, including specified parameters of reproduction and performance, and determines a detailed herd turnover and status of the animals for each category within the given farm. Included in the model is also the determination of the nutritional requirements in feed doses for all categories of animals at the dairy farm. Balancing of the nutritional requirements and the nutritional content of the feed, which the breeder submitted into the model, is done automatically. Following that, the total requirements for feed as well as the storage space and litter requirements. The analysis of total production of marketable products is then, in the context of the particular farm and its characteristics, supplemented by complex evaluation of the milk production costs. The idea behind the application is to not only evaluate the existing state but to provide also an analysis of possible changes, which the farmer is considering or forced to implement. The accuracy, independence and timeliness of business analyses is always based on the relevancy of input parameters, but also on their character, which is that of a business plan. In communicating the idea of individual farm economy evaluation, the authors of the application consider this fact to be a decisive influence and therefore neither data nor any other information are archived or otherwise processed at this stage.

benefits: Better interaction between farmer and advisor

description.name: Felling Decision Tool
description.title: Decision Support for Forest Harvesting
description.description: The Felling Decision Tool was developed to provide owners with information on estimated timber revenues and forest characteristics (wood volumes per hectare, tree top height, and mean tree size in cubic metres) at different stages in the forest cycle. This tool may be used to assist in deciding when to clearfell, by providing financial information on the implication of felling earlier or later than when the financial returns are normally at their highest. This may enable landowners to get a better return from their investment in forestry, as well as providing the revenue needed to cover reforestation costs to continue the forest cycle.

description.name: Dairy Cattle Rationing Tool - Rantsoentool Melkvee
description.title: Web tool for dairy farmers to calculate the ration for suckling dairy cows.
description.description:
The ration tool is a tool for dairy farmers,  with which the ration for lactating dairy cows can be calculated. The tool also contains an extensive list of feed materials with average feed value to simulate rations without their own analysis. 
Farmers can give better rations thank to this tool, and it is often used a controle on what companies that sell food propose as ration. 
Farmers can use it both ways: with a less costly ration, do the cows give the same amount of milk? Or if I want them to give more milk, what effect does this have on the price of the ration? It is also possible to choose to include less protein in the ration, to have less emissions, which is better for the climate.

description.name: Toolbox of good practices in digitalization

description.description:
This database and search engine is developed by the Spanish Ministry of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food. 
It collects 87 DATS applied to agriculture, food, livestock, rural development, and forestry.
They are classified by sector, subsector, topic and technology to facilitate user search. Each technology is described in detail: provider, degree of implantation (research phase or available on the market), interoperability, support materials, contact person, investment cost and potential savings, among others.

description.name: R-sim
description.title: A simulator to assess the risk of resistance developing according to its herbicidal practices
description.description:
After having chosen a rotation among the 9 proposed, and one to three weeds present in the plot, the user enters the herbicidal practices for each crop and some data of agronomic practices.

As a result R-sim provides a level of risk for each crop, and overall for rotation. It also indicates whether agronomic practices increase this risk or on the contrary decrease it.

Finally, R-sim offers herbicide strategies for each rotation, making it possible to limit the risk of the appearance of resistant weeds. R-sim is also the OAD (decision-making aid tool) for the support plan for varieties tolerant to herbicides (HTV, rapeseed or sunflower varieties Clearfield® and sunflower ExpressSun) which brings together the signatories to the charter: Technical institutes, Coop de France, National Trade Federation, UFS (seed companies) and UIPP (plant protection manufacturers). It meets two objectives: to support the advice for the sale of its varieties in order to assess the risk and to provide monitoring of practices (registration for investigation on a user account) in order to assess whether the use of these varieties is a factor increased risk.

description.name: Teagasc GIS Map of the Month
description.title: GIS maps of geographical, social, demographic, climate data and historic trends at national and regional (county) to help pan and implement advisory and development services.
description.description:
Spatial analysis research, comprising Geographic Information Systems (GIS) and earth observing (EO) remote sensing (RS) technologies provides a powerful platform for the comprehensive analysis of all land–based activities at the interface between the agri-food sector, economy and environment.
Teagasc research staff in the Spatial Analysis, Food and Agri Innovation Department provide support to industry, policy makers and farm advisors by providing detailed mapping of geographical, social and demographic data. One new map is selected as the map of the month and published on the Teagasc public website (www.teagasc.ie ). These maps help advisors target services and plan campaigns as well as providing useful local background information on long-term trends. These have been used to plan advisory progammes related to forestry, locations of farmed peat soils and development potential. Recently 2018 maps provided useful background information on feed shortages due to weather. They form an important part in supporting targeted measures related to environmental sustainability and mitigation of gaseous emissions from agriculture. Even more recently a map was used to identify geographic areas where the at risk age category for Covid 19 was highest.

This makes calculating the actual net storage capacity of a tank complex. This tool is designed to make the calculation a much simpler and quicker task. The user selected from the various menus to input the information for their exiting or proposed tank and it will give them a site specific calculation on the number of animals which the tank can hold.

description.description:
The Glyphosate Resource Center objective is to make available to the entire agricultural profession the existing solutions to get out of glyphosate. The Glyphosate Resource Center is an interactive platform that presents the main existing alternatives to glyphosate and more generally to herbicides. 
The resources are available and presented by major production sector, such as field crops, viticulture, arboriculture, vegetables, horticulture/medicinal plants and tropical cultures. Resources include review articles for a given use, data sheets on a specific alternative, feedback, videos, etc. 
Whether you are a farmer, an advisor, a teacher or a student interested in herbicide-saving weed management, you can use this tool to become a contributor and share your questions or experiences in the discussion forum.

description.name: Teagasc Client Relationship Module (CRM)
description.title: The new Teagasc Client Relationship Module (CRM) will be used by advisors to manage client interactions and diaries etc.
description.description:
Client management and advisor diary components for Teagasc are handled in a new Client Relationship Module (CRM). The CRM system is a Microsoft product and can therefore be integrated with MS Outlook, Document Management System etc. 

Teagasc advisors use the system to complete a diary and expense module. Advisors use the Diary module to report their main activities during the week whereas they use the Expense module to enter expenses against a specific week.  Once all activities are accounted for, advisors submit their diaries for approval. The system will be developed over time to make it easier for advisors to interact with clients. 

In the short term the new CRM system will:
• Continue as a client management system as currently used.
• Provide for improved management of contact details, - removal of duplicates and better management of farms, farm family members and students etc.
• Enable improved communication and management of groups for electronic communication.
• Improve efficiency of diary management and alignment with MS calendar
• Be used to meet new requirements such as communication and management of new schemes with tight deadlines.

In the medium term the new CRM system will:
• Allow for improved event management campaigns
• Allow for further integration with other systems and websites and allow for improved analysis of client data and service requirements
• Streamline the management of delivery of services to clients by different advisors across different business units.
• Improve continuity for advisors and clients when there is staff movement or absence.
• Facilitates mobile access – the CRM platform will be accessible via a range of of mobile devices and will be able to be accessed on farm by advisors
• Provide the basis for providing information to clients via a client portal where clients can “selfserve” information

In the long term the new CRM system will:
• Support “One Teagasc” by providing a single system centred around the customer/client which can hold an organisation wide record of all interactions with a client, and allow the recording of clients’ interests so that anyone in the organisation can understand the full context of the client’s relationship with the organisation, and communications can be tailored specifically for client interests and delivered through the media preferred by the client.

description.name: CAP support calculator
description.title: CAP support calculator
description.description:
As a result of the reform of the Common Agricultural Policy (CAP), the system of direct payments has changed significantly since 2015. A number of new, mandatory and voluntary elements have been added to the system. The structure of the direct support system set up in accordance with the decisions of the Hungarian Member States is illustrated in the figure below:  You can use the most relevant support calculator of the National Chamber of Agriculture to calculate the expected amount of your direct support (SAPS, greening, support for young farmers, coupled support). Based on the calculated result, you can consider joining the support scheme for small producers, which was introduced as an alternative to the single area payment scheme in 2015, and the support
available under the scheme will replace all direct support.The calculator starts with the data of the farm manager and in the end it will show if the respondent is entitled to the additional support which can be applied for by young farmers. In the Crop Production block, you can enter your important data for the support . 
In the animal husbandry section, you can provide relevant data to livestock support entitlements. 
The calculator also gives you an idea of the expected payments financed from other national sources, which you will be able to use in addition to direct payments in the single area payment scheme and in addition to small producer support in the small producer support system. Without wishing to be exhaustive, the calculator only takes into account subsidies financed from the most typical other national sources of crop and livestock production. 
The grant amounts are estimates. In the case of grants paid from EU funds, the amount of the
grant actually depends on the forint / euro exchange rate set for the payment of the grant, the actual specific grant amount (s) and any deductions. In the case of grants paid from national sources, the amount of grants to be paid depends on the amounts included in the approved national budget.

description.name: WELLTRANS
description.title: animal-friendly chicken transport
description.description:
Reserachers of Ghent University and ILVO established a protocol to evaluate the welfare of broiler chickens during the pre-slaughter phase. The protocol includes nine welfare indicators that include how severely the animals have suffered from heat or cold stress, constriction of wings, toes or heads, and so on. The evaluation of all indicators results in a general welfare index. 

Transport to the slaughterhouse can be a very unpleasant experience for broiler chickens. In order to improve welfare during that journey, researchers from UGent and ILVO developed an evaluation protocol that uses a welfare index to indicate how much or little the animals have suffered. From two experiments, an observational study, an inventory of the current transport situation and interviews with people from the sector, a practical protocol was developed that can be used in the slaughterhouse.

A total of nine welfare indicators were defined, all of which can be evaluated at the slaughterhouse by a trained person. The first part is a welfare evaluation at the end of the waiting period at the slaughterhouse, which looks at the occurrence of heat or cold stress, constrictions (wings, toes, heads), chickens with spreader legs, displacement (one chicken on top of another) and so-called back girders. The aim is to observe approximately sixty crates or drawers per hen.

Then the prevalence of fractures and bruises on the slaughter line will be determined. For each slaughter line indicator the target is to observe 10 minutes. The implementation of the complete protocol takes about 60 minutes per tome transported. After the evaluation of all indicators, it is intended that the quotations are merged into a score that serves as a general welfare index. For this purpose,

"This welfare index makes it possible to identify specific animal welfare bottlenecks. In addition, the welfare index makes it possible to compare different transports, but also different slaughterhouses, catching teams, poultry farmers, and so on. The score can serve as a guide to implement and evaluate welfare improvements, and can possibly even be used as the basis for an animal welfare label in which only meat that meets a certain score can be sold".

description.name: GEEP
description.title: Operational tool for technical management of environmental impacts.
description.description:
The GEEP tool is accessible, (1) to any breeder already involved in monitoring their technical performance (GTE) and wishing to determine and monitor their environmental performance in addition; (2) any adviser wishing to support breeders in this process (GEEP relay).

The tool offers 9 quantitative environmental performance indicators: water and energy consumption, ammonia and greenhouse gas emissions, nitrogen and phosphorus emissions, production of waste.

These indicators are complementary to the Technical-Economic Management (GTE) and Technical Management of Sow Herds (GTTT) indicators.

Their format (quantitative indicator, expressed in a common unit) allows comparison between farms.

A web interface (http://geep.ifip.asso.fr) allows an annual calculation of environmental indicators and the identification of good environmental practices to progress.

The network is "private": the data and results of individual indicators are confidential and the collective results are only accessible to members of the network.

description.name: Poderi
description.title: Poderi
description.description:
Poderi is a web-app for supporting farmers and advisors in (i) optimizing the use of agricultural inputs, (ii) keeping track of field monitoring and agricultural operations, (iii) communication.
Poderi allows each user to insert many farms and for each farm to design the fields in a map. Decision Support Systems (DSS) for fertilization, irrigation and pest control are available for each field.
Moreover, Poderi integrated sessions: (i) to register monitoring of pest presence or phenology; (ii) to insert data of soil analysis, (iii) to register the agricultural operations.
Poderi allows the farmers to share the access to their farms with the advisors, with the possibility also of chatting with them. Moreover, the advisors can chat, send notifications and exchange data with all their associated farmers.
The web-app was originally focused on olive growing, but it is easily expandable to other crops, so today the DSS are available for the following crops: olive, grapevine and maize.

description.name: EASE (Egnos sAvingS in agriculturE)
description.title: EASE (Egnos sAvingS in agriculturE) provides farmers with cost-benefit analyses on the use of EGNOS for machinery guidance in some of their typical agricultural labours.
description.description:
EASE is based on a comprehensive (but pragmatic) model, in order to try to be simple but also in line with reality. The methodology takes into account the specific circumstances, considering both costs and labour practices, of each farmer. The tool can perform scalable studies, including as many operation tasks as the farmer considers relevant. In the end, the user can print or export the results obtained. EASE is recommended for those types of crops that do not require very high precision solutions, i.e. extensive crops in dry areas, such as dryland cereals, legumes and sunflowers.

EASE presents a user-friendly interface that guides the user smoothly along the three configuration screens before obtaining the final EGNOS savings of the proposed scenario. To facilitate the configuration tasks, explanation pop ups are also available for all the data fields (look for the exclamation mark bubbles). In addition, the text of EASE can be automatically translated to any language (check the top right widget). Anyway, a detailed user manual is also at the disposal of the user for further consultation.

description.name: Agrecalc
description.title: Agrecalc is a carbon calculator that measures and benchmarks greenhouse gas emissions of a wide range of agricultural systems.
description.description:
Agrecalc is a carbon footprint tool developed by SAC Consulting together with SRUC researchers. It enables farm enterprises to identify and measure emissions, benchmark key performance indicators, identify mitigation strategies and monitor improvements.

Our mission and vision is to be one-of-a kind tool for carbon emission calculation, benchmarking and mitigation, enabling the world’s food supply chain to reduce its carbon footprint.

We also support transition to low-carbon agriculture, helping deliver net-zero emissions from food production while increasing the level of both efficiency and farm productivity.

Agrecalc only employs the latest validated scientific methodologies and encourages practical mitigation measures and produces precise carbon footprint reports for all farm enterprises. The coefficients that Agrecalc uses are those of the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC), the internationally accepted authority on climate change. The IPCC is a part of the United Nations (UN) that was established in 1988 and was presented with a Nobel Peace Prize in 2007.

Developed within Scotland's Rural College (SRUC), a world-renowned institution providing sector-leading research, education and consultancy focused on rural, environmental and land-based activities, the carbon calculation tool combines more than 100 years of agricultural science, and more than 15 years of practical farming experience, delivered by SAC Consulting, the commercial arm that supports and advises land-based businesses in the rural economy to optimise agricultural outcomes and supply chains, always in balance with the environment and in line with national agricultural policy.

Agrecalc is also accredited to PAS:2050 standard: Greenhouse Gas Emissions of Products (PAS 2050:2011) is an independent and widely recognised standard providing requirements and guidelines on specific issues relevant for carbon footprints, including land-use change, carbon uptake, biogenic carbon emissions, soil carbon change, and green electricity.

The platform allows the user to run, compare and simulate scenarios to understand the impact of on-farm changes and monitor progress. The data the user gets back from Agrecalc reports allows them to identify the areas where they can take practical action to reduce their carbon foot print and boost the resource efficiency of their business.

The new version of the platform, with an improved interface and user experience is expected by the end of 2022.

The database provides basic information from individual advisors (business, services, geographic, sector and other expertise/competence and also links to social media (LinkedIn). This allows advisors to promote their own profile based on their work and experience. The justification for the database is that more and more we hear that many independent advisors do not have easy access to new information from other advisors, research and other support services and it is expected that advisors can use the database for networking and to enhance their activities within their AKIS.

description.name: IMAGO
description.title: Toolbox for region-specific rural development
description.description:
IMAGO stands for Integrated Method for Action-oriented Regional Development. The toolbox - a set of methods and tools - can be used by coordinators of region-specific rural development processes to improve the process they are involved with. The focus is on rural development processes related to the management of open space. How do you get an overview of all projects in open space in a certain area? How can these projects be better coordinated with each other? How can rural actors work together?

IMAGO offers five tools for regio-specific rural development. 

EXPLORE: "The EXPLORE tool helps users to get a good picture of their working area, both spatially and socio-economically. The tool provides four tools (actor analysis, project inventory, aerial photographs and maps) to get to know the actors, projects, developments, opportunities and threats of open space in your area".
DEBATE: "This tool opens up the debate on open space with municipal officials and politicians. It makes users think about open space and the policies they pursue. The tool is based on three debating sessions and provides tools to process the results of those sessions".
PLAY: "Visionary is a method to interactively discuss the opportunities and threats to open space in a region. The PLAY tool offers two versions of Visionary: a general version for awareness raising and a customised version for vision development. Visionary is based on the serious game Participology of Birmingham City University".
COMBINE: "The COMBINE tool provides a handy overview of all open space instruments in Flanders. The atlas is not exhaustive, but very comprehensive. It also offers support to use or combine instruments".
EVALUATE: "This tool helps you reflect on region-specific actions in open space. What is the impact on an area? Are actors missing? The tool is divided into a meta-evaluation, which you carry out before the start of a new project, and an evaluation of an ongoing initiative".

description.name: Slurry Tank Calibration online calculator
description.title: Slurry tank calibration tool
description.description:
A slurry tank operator can use the app to calibration the rate of slurry application. The calculator can be opened on the users phone while in the field (internet access required). They input the variables using four sliders, for size of tanker, spread width, time to empty tank and then the desired application rate. (The time to empty the tank will be recorded by the operator on the first load)
The calculator will then give the driver the required forward speed needed to achieve the desired application rate.

description.name: Registration of Phytosanitary Products
description.title: Database providing information on authorised plant protection products, approved active substances, instructions for registration of protection products and maximum residue limits
description.description:
It consists of 5 sections that allow information to be obtained on the phytosanitary products authorised in Spain, active substances approved for their manufacture, instructions for the registration of phytosanitary products and maximum residue limits in plant products and documents on the official recognition of tests.

The section on phytosanitary products contains 4 menus that allow searches for phytosanitary products by registration number, trade name, active substance, crop and crop/plant, also allowing consultation of movements that have taken place in the Register of Phytosanitary Products between two dates. As the databases do not allow to ensure that the computer sweeps analyse details such as certain exceptional restrictions that may eventually be included in the authorisations, it has been foreseen that the user will have the possibility to have on screen a display of the complete resolution of the registration of the selected product with all the specific conditions established.

The section on approved active substances provides information on those authorised for use in the manufacture of plant protection products.

The updating of the database at 30-day intervals helps to make accurate, reliable and up-to-date information available to all users of this page, which will undoubtedly help greatly in making the use of Plant Protection Products more responsible, safer, more effective and increasingly more respectful of the environment.

The section on instructions for the registration of plant protection products has been designed to include documents of interest within the scope of the Official Register of Plant Protection Products and Equipment.

The section on maximum residue limits includes a compilation with all the current levels in Spain for plant products.

description.name: Relative Value calculator for ruminant feed ingredients
description.title: Feed ingredient prices are compared with 2 common feed ingredients to calculate the relative value
description.description:
This is an online calculator which is simple to use once you know the cost price of different feed ingredients which are available as feed supplements. It compares the energy and protein content of grains or feed ingredients (straights) to 2 standard feed ingredients (option 1 higher protein requirement) barley and soybean meal  or (option 2 lower protein requirement ) barley and distillers grains to calculate the relative value of those products. The calculator is very useful in to determine the relative value of alternative feeds fed as ingredients in mixtures or as straight feeds used as buffer feed to supplement grass or silage diets. The calculator can also be used to compare the cost of bulky low dry matter feeds and forages, this needs to done with some caution as to the higher handling costs and variability of these ingredients.
Users should always factor in their own specific costs of transport, storage, rolling, processing and mixing in the costs 

A spreadsheet option is available in MS Excel.

description.title: Training, events and workshops in Castilla la Mancha
description.description: The general objectives of IRIAF focus on the need to promote research, innovation and development, as well as dissemination and technical advice, in the field of agriculture, livestock, agri-food industries and forestry in order to achieve greater efficiency and productivity in the agri-food sector, making the production of safe and quality food compatible with adequate protection of the environment, with forest management, sustainable forest management, evaluation and conservation of forest resources, conservation of biodiversity , biotechnology, the fight against climate change, all with a vocation for the future, generating employment and prosperity for the region as a whole.

description.name: iVeg
description.title: IoT-based Platform for Greenhouse Crop Production
description.description:
The cloud architecture used for the development is divided into layers, which allow us to work on the development in a decoupled way. These are divided in the following way: Context Producers, Back-end and Front-end. The main advantage of this architecture is to allow independent developments in each of the layers so that the operation of the previous layer is not affected. 

LAYER 0 - CONTEXT PRODUCERS is responsible for generating all the context information. To generate this information, it is necessary to have physical devices capable of collecting information from the environment. These devices are data acquisition system with sensors and actuators. These IoT devices lack a standard in the data format, and, for this reason, it was decided to use the standard of FIWARE NGSI trying to homogenize the data format. Other of the available context producing systems is the use of weather forecast public service allowing to make consultations in real time, 76 hours in the future and historical data in a determined region. In this way, we provide knowledge of the external climate conditions of the greenhouse without the need to have our own acquisition system in the farm.

Layer 1 – back-end is known as the data access layer or the logical part of any application. It is responsible for performing the heavy tasks of the system. It focuses on performing tasks such as data extraction from layer 0, CRON processes, context information managers, REST API services, language interpreters, databases, control systems, among others. The benefit of having a decoupled Back-end is to allow new developments, avoiding downtime in the rest of the services. In the same way, it allows developing new Front-end clients without affecting the current one. Another benefit is to provide an intermediate security layer in the system. 
The core of the platform is the Greenhouse Model as a Service (GMaaS). This service is based on Matlab Production Server. When a greenhouse is registered in the system, specific models for inside climate, crop production, and irrigation are implemented in the platform as particular services. These model-based services provide the user a DSS tool to obtain forecast about indoor climate conditions, crop production, or irrigation needs, among others.

description.name: linkconsult toolkit on interactive innovation
description.title: tools to progress innovations in groups
description.description:
For all 7(!) stages of an innovation, practical suggestions are given to optimise the chance to proceed, to combine warm and cold processes, to 'clear the air' in the relations, to alighn the roles of partners, analyse the network of the project and it's environment (ecosystem). Cards are available (mail Eelke)
The toolkit is used in i2connect training and CECRA16 training of advisors

description.name: Terranimo
description.title: Terranimo - simulation of the compaction risk due to agricultural machinery
description.description: Terranimo is a freely accessible online computer model that predicts the risk of soil compaction by farm machinery. Terranimo is primarily addressed to farmers, agricultural contractors and consultants, and enforcement authorities. The simulation model calculates the risk of compaction damage for real operating conditions. The results can serve to optimise the use of agricultural machines, and thus help to avoid damage to the soil structure.

functionalities: Sustainable food production and healthy diets

description.name: Fertiliser Spreader Calibration
description.title: Fertiliser spreader calibration calculator
description.description:
This online calculator is designed for use with older spreader or spreaders where calibration apps are not available. The calculator will give the operator the desired flow rate of the spreader which is then compared to the actual flow rate which is measured during calibration. 
The operator using the sliders to input the forward speed, bout width and the desired application rate. The desired flow rate is then displayed in the results.

description.name: Simulareg
description.title: Simulareg is a training tool that provides a test environment to check the behavior of a crop or a virtual plot relationed to a specific irrigation schedule.
description.description:
The aim of the simulator is educational and it provides a test environment to check the behavior of a crop or a virtual plot relationed to a specific irrigation schedule. It is possible to define parameters like the weather, the soil type and the irrigation system for the virtual plot and it allows the configuration of different irrigation strategies for the schedule.
Once the plot and the schedule have been defined, the theorical results can be simulated and displayed in a graphical way where users can compare their results with the results obtained by an expert.Thanks to the data comparison between user and expert results, users are able to learn better irrigation techniques.

description.name: Plataforma Tierra
description.title: An open innovation community, training and tools
description.description:
Plataforma Tierra is an open and participatory community of producers, companies, and technology developers, where experiences, content of interest, sectoral reports, market and conjuncture reports, and digital tools are shared to aid in the transition towards Agriculture 4.0. 
Additionally, it is a platform that fosters both face-to-face and online training with courses, webinars, and events specialized in various aspects of the agri-food sector. Among the free digital tools we offer, there is a Farming Management Information System, tools for predicting irrigation based on weather prediction models and specific variables for each crop, and for creating specific fertilization plans by crop and phenology. 
We also offer more than 700 market indicators, including product prices, marketing, exports, production, and other metrics.
And as experts, we provide advisory services to the agro-industry.

description.name: DiseñEN
description.title: A tool to design ecological infrastructures and to serve as a meeting point for sharing experiences related to conservation biological control
description.description:
DiseñEN is an interactive web tool that aims to support decision making for the implementation of conservation biological control strategies and to offer tailor-made solutions. The tool is able to design a hedge made up of native plants adapted to particular conditions based on a few parameters, such as the available space in metres or the location, offering the farmer an optimised design for his/her farm. The selection of the plant species that will make up the hedge is carried out in such a way as to introduce functional biodiversity, which allows the agro-ecosystems to be adapted to the needs of the native beneficial auxiliary fauna and thus promote biological control by conservation.
Anyone, with no need for knowledge of native flora and its auxiliary fauna, can obtain a list of suitable plant species, the number of plants of each species and a planting sketch. 
The website also contains information on native plant species that have desirable attributes to host natural enemies of pests, as well as on auxiliary fauna that can help in pest control, including information not only on arthropods, but also on vertebrates (reptiles, birds and mammals).

description.name: COFORD GROFOR Dynamic Yield Models
description.title: Dynamic Yield Models for Ireland
description.description: Irish dynamic yield models are being developed for the most important commercial species. This necessitates the models being used through a computer programme rather than from yield tables, as is the practice with static yield models. The key advantage of dynamic yield models is the flexibility and accuracy that they give to forest managers. Models can be applied to actual plantation characteristics rather than trying to align the plantation to a set management regime outlined in a yield table.

description.name: Knowledge platform for rural areas and fishing
description.title: Tool to "serve knowledge", which will undoubtedly boost the development of our own technological system.
description.description:
The Ministry of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food understands that a significant effort must be made so that companies in the agricultural, fishing and food sectors, agricultural organizations and socio-economic agents in rural areas are equipped with well-trained people who promote a efficient relationship with the centers that generate knowledge.

The variety of our agriculture, our fishing system and our agri-food activity contain and can generate, if adequately supported by knowledge centers, a volume of information and a first-rate technological arsenal.

It is necessary that the knowledge generating centers orient their activity to satisfy the demand for this knowledge, formulated by the companies and the social agents of the rural environment.

These reflections motivated the Ministry of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food to develop the "Knowledge Platform for the rural and fishing environment" as a tool to "serve knowledge", which will undoubtedly promote the development of our own technological system.

Knowledge about the rural environment must also flow to the rest of society, mostly urban. We must not miss the opportunity to contribute to shaping the paradigms that are decisively installed in society and that, to a large extent, condition the future of our agriculture, our fishing system and our agri-food system.

benefits: Better interaction between farmer and advisor

description.description: For registered wheat plots, downloads are available for daily values and infection-risk maps as well as forecasts of DON contamination. In addition, an ad hoc risk assessment for any wheat plot in Switzerland is available. The site of the plot is automatically located by the smartphone GPS.

description.name: FEGA website
description.title: Website that is available to citizens and companies so that they can access information and the services and electronic procedures of the Public Administration.
description.description: The Spanish Agrarian Guarantee Fund, OA (FEGA) is an autonomous body of those provided for in article 98 of Law 40/2015, of October 1, on the Legal Regime of the Public Sector, attached to the Ministry of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food (MAPA) through the General Secretariat of Agriculture and Food, whose main mission is to make the EAGF (European Agricultural Fund for Agricultural Guarantee) and FEADER (European Agricultural Fund for Rural Development) Common Agricultural Policy (CAP) funds. assigned to Spain, are strictly applied to achieve the objectives of this policy, effectively reaching beneficiaries who meet the requirements established for their concession, within the deadlines provided in regulatory regulations, promoting a homogeneous application of aid the CAP in the entire territory of the State, avoiding fraud and minimizing the risks of financial corrections derived from the incorrect management of said Funds

description.name: Cattle Youngstock Tool - JongveeTool
description.title: Web tool for dairy farmers that monitors the growth trajectory of young cattle.
description.description:
In the free Cattle Youngstock Tool, you register the weight or chest size of your young cattle. The Cattle Youngstock Tool then illustrates how well your farm scores in relation to the target trajectory. With this tool you can also evaluate the growth trajectory and growth rate of different age groups at a glance. 
By registering weight or chest circumference on a regular basis, you can detect errors early on in the rearing process and make adjustments in order to realise an optimal growth trajectory. This ultimately results in high-yielding heifers that calve on time.
keywords: Young cattle – chest circumference – weight – growth curve – insemination age prediction

LK digital is an online-based blog which tries to collect any content like articles, videos and farm stories related to digital topics of production, farm management, policies and training services.
It is designed to be the central entering point for farmers and other stakeholders who aim to learn about smart farming and related fields of agricutlure.
LK digital was created in 2017 as part of an educational project under Austrian national RD programme.

description.name: All Ireland Roundwood Production Forecast
description.title: All Ireland Roundwood Production Forecast
description.description: The All Ireland Roundwood Production Forecast 2016-2035 was launched in 2016 by Department of Agriculture, Food and the Marine. Three forecasts were presented in the report: (1) forecast of gross volume, (2) forecast of net realisable volume which includes reductions for harvest loss, accessibility and crops unlikely to be harvested and (3) forecast of wood fibre availability. A GIS portal was developed to accompany the forecast that generates user defined spatial forecasts up to 2035 for private and Coillte forests. The portal facilitates the dissemination of comprehensive volume forecast information on the national forest estate in an accessible, reproducible and transparent way.

description.name: IKMIS
description.title: A system of integrated plant protection information, consulting and training
description.description: IKMIS is a system of integrated plant protection information, consulting, and training, consisting of 4 free electronic services for farmers, advisers, researchers, and anyone interested in agriculture. The aim of the system is to inform the Lithuanian agricultural sector about the occurrence of harmful organisms in crops, to improve their skills and to speed up optimal decisions on the use of plant protection products, thus reducing the negative impact on the environment, human and animal health in Lithuania.  The system promotes both a sparing use of plant protection products and a use of non-chemical integrated pest management techniques. Data from insect traps, online weather stations and weather forecasts are integrated and automatically fed into the system. The data is used to recommend when not to spray fields, up-to-date information on the spread of harmful organisms in different crops in all regions of Lithuania is provided, as well as timely interactive advice. During the growing season, farmers' crops are monitored, plant surveys are carried out according to a methodology developed by scientists, and data and recommendations are provided by the plant production advisers of the Advisory Service. To ensure the quality of the advice, unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), satellite data and nitrogen demand measurement devices are used to determine the health status of crops. If you observe your crop and suspect a disease or pest is spreading, but are unable to identify the pest or disease, you can find all the information you need in the system. The catalogues of diseases, pests and weeds are published in IKMIS to help identify the disease or pest more quickly and to make it easier to choose the plant protection products registered in Lithuania to control them. The catalogues are continuously updated by researchers at the Lithuanian Research Centre for Agriculture and Forestry, and the information in the plant protection product database is directly delivered from the database of the State Plant Production Service. Farms using plant protection products must comply with the requirements of Integrated Pest Management. By filling in a questionnaire in the system, each user can find out whether the farm complies with these requirements and, if not, is given information on areas for improvement. If advice is needed on the application of integrated pest management practices, an adviser can be contacted via the IKMIS system. Another important service of IKMIS is training. Farmers have to continuously improve their qualifications and knowledge through various courses, trainings, and seminars, which are not always organised in the area where they live, thus they have to travel to other towns. With the IKMIS distance learning service – which consists of more than 15 training programmes – farmers will be able to learn at home, in a way and at a time that suits them best. This way they can save their valuable time and money. Most importantly, almost all IKMIS services (except distance learning) are free of charge and available to anyone with internet access.

description.description: The tool is a platform for remote advisory events. Farm tours, lectures and presentations can be recorded by the advisor and hosted on the platform with supporting documents for farmers to access. Video calls can also take place between advisors and groups of farmers on the platform.

description.name: Earnings Tool - VerdienWijzer
description.title: The Earnings Tool is an online tool te inspire farmers on the possibilities for earning models for their specific business.
description.description:
This interactive tool makes it clear to the agricultural entrepreneurs that their customers do not (only) pay for a product, but for delivered value.
It analyses the underlying value(s) delivered for each possible sub-activity of an agricultural farm or company. The tool users use simple selection criteria to discover which adjustments to their current earnings model are most logically within reach, and which values match their personal motives (‘what gives you energy?') and those of their (intended) customers.

description.name: OLEOdiag
description.title: Winter rapeseed accident diagnostic tool
description.description:
OLEOdiag, a diagnostic aid tool, takes stock of winter oilseed rape accidents through more than 400 descriptive sheets and 1000 photographs. This new tool brings together all of Terres Inovia's experience to help you identify the disease, pest or accident that damages your rapeseed. It also gives access to Terres Inovia's advice for an even more complete service.
Diagnosis is based on stage, organ and symptom observed. The dynamic consultation allows you to reach the desired accident in just 4 clicks.

An illustrated sheet then gives you:
- additional information to confirm or refine your diagnosis
- since February 2008, access to Terres Inovia's advice on the accident concerned.

OLEOdiag also offers to search for an accident based on its suspected cause. You can then find out about all the possible manifestations of an accident.

description.name: Meat Cattle Rationing Tool – Rantsoentool Vleesvee
description.title: Web tool for beef cattle farmers to calculate the ration for the main categories of meat cattle (bulls, young cattle, suckling cows).
description.description:
The ration tool is a tool for beef cattle farmers, with which the ration for meat cows can be calculated. The tool also contains an extensive list of feed materials with average feed value to simulate rations without their own analysis. It allows farmers to give better rations and is often used to check what companies, which sell food, offer as rations.
Farmers can use it both ways: 
- with a less costly ration, do the cows give the same amount of meat?
- or if I want them to give more meat, what effect does this have on the price of the ration?
It is also possible to choose to include less protein in the ration, to have less emissions, which is better for the climate.
keywords: Ration - beef cattle - feed materials - feed value

agricultural_sectors: Meat (cattle, pigs, sheep, goats, poultry, rabbits, snails)

description.name: Spraytech - Spuithulp
description.title: Spraytech web tool for better choices in spray technique and less diffuse pollution when using pesticides
description.description:
The tool helps to immediately make the right decisions about spray nozzles, pressure, buffer zones, product, etc. – all in function of the crop and the problem (pests or disease). For farmers/horticulturists and professional users of pesticides, the tool provides practical help to spray more efficiently, with less overrun, and less environmental pressure. The tool is based on a series of large background databases full of information such as the characteristics of each existing commercial spray nozzle and every pesticide approved for use.
 It helps farmers to make the right choices in terms of:
- Spray technology to be used (type of sprayer, nozzle size and type, pressure, speed of driving) 
- Product to be used
- Buffer zone required along a waterway
The three factors – three circles in the web tool, fuelled by databases – are all coupled to each other.

In the circles 'Spraying technique' and 'Product' , the user can go through various scenarios step-by-step via dropdowns. The 'Buffer zone' sphere always indicates the width of the buffer zone along a watercourse to be respected based on the chosen product and in combination with the chosen spraying technique.

description.name: Biorisk
description.title: Biorisk, digital biosecurity to protect your animals
description.description:
Biorisk is a ground-breaking biosecurity system that aims to stop the spread of diseases on swine farms.

Using an easy system of movement receptors and emitters, Biorisk helps to control potentially dangerous staff movements through the farm so that risky ones that could contribute to the spread of pathogens can be avoided or limited.

HOW BIORISK WORKS

-	Step 1: Our staff use the facility floor plan to identify the optimal locations for movement receptor installations.

-	Step 2: We provide the devices and monitor their installarion through their wireless connections.

-	Step 3: Blood or saliva samples from the animals in each area are tested in the laboratory to establish their health status.

-	Step 4: Based on these results, we classify the areas as healthy or infected.

-	Step 5: We assign an emitter to each worker which allows data about their movements through the facility to be collected as they pass near the movement receptors.

-	Step 6: These data are processed in the cloud and analysed to detect any potentially hazardous behaviours that may spread diseases through the farm. Based on the errors detected, we then carefully coach these workers to help correct any problems identified.

-	Step 7: The system is managed via an app that allows real-time monitoring and generates the analytics required to understand the truth rather than the theory of what happens on the farm. It also generates alerts for immediate actions required to preserve or improve the farm’s health status.

-	Step 8: Biorisk promotes a system of continuous improvement, making the real outcomes of biosecurity protocols visible and allowing the early correction of errors in order to keep the site disease-free.

Simply enter many datas like the number of horseriders, the number of workers, the number of horses and economices data.

description.name: IfA Carbon Support Tool
description.title: The Carbon Support Tool helps with the selection of the most appropriate tools / programmes to calculate carbon emissions and sequestration.
description.description:
This tool compares different farm carbon assessments - it considers different enterprise combinations and land management approaches and reports each carbon programme's suitability within those contexts.

The pilot version of the tool compares 3 carbon assessment options: Farm Carbon Calculator, Agrecalc and the Cool Farm Tool, with scope to add more in future. The Carbon Support Tool invites users to select 'areas of interest' which are relevant to a farm enterprise, then asks a series of 'yes' or 'no' questions based on the assessment criteria used by the carbon assessments. The results indicate a level of best fit for each carbon assessment option included within the Carbon Support Tool. Users can create multiple assessments, and rename each of them.

description.name: Climate assessment by animal observation - Stappenplan klimaatevaluatie door dierobservatie
description.title: Web tool for pig farmers to get an overall picture of the microclimate in their department through simple observations of the pigs and their environment.
description.description: The step-by-step guide for climate assessment through animal observation is a tool that enables pig farmers to obtain an overall picture of the state of the microclimate in their stable through simple observations of the pigs and their environment. On the basis of these observations, pig farmers receive specific advice on how to optimise the climate in the stable. For each type of ventilation, you will also be given a practical guide on how the climate can be optimised by means of a film.

description.name: Agfood.ie
description.title: The Department of Agriculture, Food and the Marine provides a range of online services to farmers and their approved advisors (agents)
description.description:
This is the online portal for farmers and advisors to interact with the Irish Ministry of Agriculture (DAFM) all advisors gain agent access to their farmer clients through a prior approval system.
The portal includes
1. Basic Payment Scheme
2. Animal Identification and Movement System
3. Forestry Services
4. Financial Self-Services (FSS)
5. Other Services


1. Basic Payment Scheme (CAP income support pillar 1.)
-make applications online
-edit maps online relating your land parcels / plots
-greening requirements on Crop Diversification and Ecological Focus Areas (EFA)
-information on entitlements


2. Animal Identification and Movement System
-calf birth registration facility
-cattle herd register facility
- cattle movements in and out
- farm to farm movement certification 

3 Forestry Services IFORIS Internet allows Registered Foresters:
-to track online the progress of any pre-approval forestry application
-to map and submit new pre-approval (Form 1) applications online for the Afforestation, FEPS and Forest Road Scheme
-to access ortho-photography for the entire country, Environmental Statutory Designations and other relevant datasets to assist with Forestry applications
-allows forest owners registered for online services with the Department to apply for the Forestry annual premium online

4. Financial Self-Services (FSS) Customers can carry out the following activities:

-view their financial data, including historical invoices and payments
-pay invoices/overpayments electronically with Debit/Credit Card (with the back-end accounts system and the bank being updated simultaneously)
-view, download and print payment remittances
-view, download and print account statements (including annual farmer statements)

5. Other Services
-provides online access to the annual nitrogen and phosphorus statements issued by the Department
-make a Nitrates Derogation Application
-license application system - licences in respect of veterinary medicines can be applied for online

description.name: Genpro Online
description.title: The online solution for stud book management
description.description:
Genpro Online is an online platform developed for recording data related to the management of Stud Books, presently being used in the management of nearly 60 breeds (of cattle, sheep, goat, horses and pigs)  in Portugal and Spain. 
Genpro Online has been developed with a modular structure, so it is easily adapted to different species and to various production systems. And also to various languages.
Pedigrees, performance reports, field sheets, breeding values: all at the distance of one click. The information can be accessed anywhere at anytime, by technicians or farmers. A policy of accesses ensures that each user only gets the information it’s supposed to.
