description.name: Sandy by Trinity AgTech
description.title: Sandy, the natural capital navigator by Trinity AgTech
description.description:
Trinity AgTech presents Sandy, a Smart Natural Capital Navigator trusted by various farms to: assess and manage their natural capital, enhance sustainability, seize new business opportunities.

Key Features of Sandy:

Measure: Provides unparalleled analytical accuracy for evaluating carbon, biodiversity, and water protection. It consolidates monitoring of sustainability, profitability, and productivity in a single platform.

Manage: Employs AI-driven predictive intelligence that aids in optimising farm management scenarios. This feature supports farms transitioning to more sustainable and profitable practices.

Monetise: Offers avenues for monetisation via provenance reporting, input optimisation, and connections to Trinity Natural Capital Markets for trading in the natural capital domain.

Sandy serves all farming systems universally, be it organic, conventional, dairy, poultry, viticulture, and more. Sandy is the first tool to accurately measure carbon stored in both upland and lowland peat. It's the only reliable solution for measuring soil carbon & natural capital in extensive grasslands & uplands. Sandy uses the most recent scientific evidence and advanced model-data fusion techniques. The tool has a user-friendly interface with reduced data entry; integrates with other farm management and accounting software. Sandy is independent from large agribusinesses, placing farmers' priorities first.

Additional Features

Biodiversity: Automate the creation of biodiversity improvement plans and trade biodiversity net gain tokens.
Agroforestry: Analyse the impact of various agroforestry practices on the land.
Water: Manage and evidence water protection strategies; optimise nitrogen use efficiency.
Provenance: Quickly generate certificates to verify the farm's credentials in carbon, biodiversity, water quality, and animal welfare.

description.name: PinUp.com
description.title: Pinup.com is an online board where you can pin virtual sticky notes
description.description:
Sometimes the best way to organise your thoughts and ideas is to stick little pieces of paper on a wall and move them around until everything is in the right order. But it can be messy or clumsy, and you might not always have the necessary materials to hand. Or a big enough area of wall to accommodate everything you want to make a note of.

Pinup.com is a free online web-based version of a board. You can create virtual sticky notes, choose their size and colour, stick them on your wall, and write onto them. Moving notes around is easy, as is changing the size of a note if you end up writing too much.

You can keep your electronic wall private, or make it public, or invite specific people to see or contribute to it. And because it's web-based, it's instantly accessible from whichever computer you happen to be using at the time.

The website is particularly useful for online participatory exercises such as a card sort where  participants can move the sticky notes and others can see in real time their contributions.

- its botanical description

- its affinity for environments and cultures

User can take stock of the different means of control available:

description.name: Global SLM Database
description.title: Global Database on Sustainable Land Management (SLM)
description.description:
The objective of documenting and assessing SLM practices is to share and spread valuable knowledge in land management, support evidence-based decision-making, and scale up identified good practices, thereby contributing to preventing and reducing land degradation and to restoring degraded land.

WOCAT, together with its Network partners, has developed a well-accepted framework and standardised tools and methods for documentation, monitoring, evaluation and dissemination of SLM knowledge, covering all steps from data collection with several questionnaires, to the Global SLM Database and to using the information for decision support.

Languages: English, French, Spanish, Russian, Chinese, Khmer, Lao, Thai, Mongolian

description.name: Web Application Potato Disease - Webapplicatie Aardappelziekte
description.title: The 'web application potato disease' enables you to get a picture at any time of the risk to which your potato parcel is exposed at a specific location and time.
description.description:
The web application potato disease gives you an insight into the relevant, current and location-specific information needed to tackle Phytopthora infestans (= potato disease) in potatoes in a reasoned manner.
➢ Relevant: the underlying database contains many calculations that simulate the development of the disease or the evolution of crop protection. From the large amount of data, only the information that supports the final decision (whether or not to protect) is shown.
➢ Current: every three hours, detailed weather data from a network of 48 weather stations in Flanders are processed in the disease model. Twice a day they are supplemented with hourly forecasts up to 96 hours ahead, per climate region.
➢ Location-based: for each postal code, the closest weather stations are taken into account.
With the 'web application potato disease' you can therefore get a picture at any time of the risk to which your potato parcel is exposed at a specific location and time. In this way, crop protection agents are used in a responsible manner.   The web application is free for PCA members. Anyone can become a member of PCA upon annual payment of the membership fee.

functionalities: Sustainable food production and healthy diets

description.name: Slaughter weight optimisation - Slachtdoordacht
description.title: This is a calculation tool that gives  an insight into the most important mechanisms to optimise slaughter weight
description.description:
The tool allows the user to gain insight into the optimal slaughter weight for the fattening pigs and the economic importance of the optimisation. The model calculates the changes in the gross balance per fattening pig delivered and per pig place per year as a function of increasing fattening time and increasing weight of the pig. The optimisation model then looks for the fattening duration with the highest gross balance per pig place per year. 

In addition, the tool also lends itself very well to analysing the effect on the gross balance of improvements in the pigs' key figures or of changes in the  market conditions. The design of the learning module is that users can learn by performing various simulations and by using different parameter values to gain insight into the effect of these changes. 

The tool has a configuration page, where the parameters for slaughter yield, manure production and weight categories, linked with its discounts and surcharges from the slaughterhouse, can be adapted for each farms' situation. On the simulation page of the tool, you can make a simulation for the set with your own parameters, adapting the animal technical data, the economic data and the simulation features (number of production rounds). The results of these simulations can be found on the result page of the tool, showing several graphs and tabels, describing optimisation,  evolutions in turnover and costs, and the animal-technical performances.

description.name: Teagasc AgileTECH Advisory Facilitation Method
description.title: Facilitation Method: Managing Grass
description.description: The AgileTECH project aimed to understand the relevance and use of existing scientific knowledge regarding grassland management among farmers. In this context, the project co-designed a method for use by advisers in a discussion group context. Sheep, beef and dairy farmers and farm advisers were involved in five focus groups, which were recorded, transcribed and analysed. The analysis informed subsequent co-design stages, involving scientists, specialists, farm advisers and graphic designers. A group facilitation method to encourage farmer engagement with grassland management was co-designed and prototyped by the group. The method reflects findings from across the focus groups conducted with farmers and advisers and contains four main elements: building farmer-adviser rapport and approaching ‘goal setting’ with clients, which was identified as a crucial first step; building ‘Managing Grass Action Plans’, which takes a flexible approach to supporting farmers to identify cost-effective, impactful and achievable actions on their farms; use of storyboards (illustrated stories) to facilitate the group to address social (relationship), cultural (pride) and economic issues associated with managing grass; and, finally, a discussion group self-evaluation method (‘health check’) to improve group functioning (organisation, trust, solidarity, learning, fun and enjoyment). The group facilitation method is presented in print and interactive PDF form, accompanied by a customised carry bag containing the co-designed resource materials required to implement the extension method. Over 20 farm advisers (Teagasc and the cooperative sector) have received training to use the method and are currently practising it and generating feedback on its use.

description.name: M- tool Drying off selectively - Selectief Droogzetten
description.title: Selectively drying off dairy, for reduced use of antibiotics
description.description:
The demand for the reduction of antibiotics in animal husbandry is getting louder and louder. On dairy farms,  drying off selectively is a way to achieve this, provided certain conditions are met.
Selective drying off consists of only drying off infected cows with antibiotics; the non-infected animals are not treated with antibiotics but must be (extra) protected against new udder infections by optimising drying off management (housing, feeding, vaccination...) and inserting an internal teat valve. Selective drying off is an option on well-managed dairy farms with low infection pressure.
A first step is therefore to take a close look at one's own operational management. Monitoring the tank milk cell count over a longer period of time is an aid to evaluate udder health throughout the farm. A tank milk cell count that is too high demands an improvement of the general udder health before applying selective drying off. If good udder health is met, the second step is to determine which cows are infected. This can be done today by means of a recently developed infection indicator. After all, we want to administer long-acting antibiotics to cows with a high risk of infection at the time of drying off, while cows with a low risk of infection can be dryed off without long-acting antibiotics. It is always recommended to protect the cows with an internal teat valve.
With an educational module you can check to what extent your farm is eligible to selectively dry off cows. In addition, based on the MPR data, you can estimate for each individual cow how likely it is that she will be infected with a harmful mastitis agent at the end of lactation. This information helps to make the right drying decision in consultation with the veterinarian supervising the farm.

description.name: IPM Decisions Platform
description.title: Decision Support Systems for Integrated Pest Management
description.description:
Having accurate and reliable information about pest and disease risk is essential when planning integrated pest management strategies.

The IPM Decisions Platform provides a “one stop shop” for decisions support in integrated pest management. It gives farmers and advisers access to a large range of existing Decision Support Systems for their regional conditions. The platform consists of a web-based framework that provides farmers, advisors and researchers access to a wide range of decision support systems and weather data from across Europe through a set of four dashboards. It provides farmers, advisors and researchers with the ability to assess the reliability and suitability of different decision support systems so they can select those that are most suited to their needs. 

The dashboard provides an “at-a-glance” indication of the risk for pest and diseases across whole farm using a traffic light colour scheme. Farmers and advisors are able to select crops, pests and diseases of interest using a set of drop down menus. The dashboard also includes the ability to set up email or text alerts for each of the pest and diseases.

The dashboard allows farmers and advisors to compare the results from a number of different decision support systems for a given pest or disease, so that they can choose the systems that are right for them.

The IPM Decisions Platform allows access to decision support systems from across Europe. Many of these decisions support systems will have been developed for specific countries. This dashboard allows researchers to modify the parameters within the decision support systems to adapt them for use across a wider range of countries. Researcher are able to compare the outputs before and after modification of the parameters. The dashboard also allows researchers to download the results for comparison with field data for validation and verification purposes.

The time-limited element to view varied resources, videos, infographics etc. followed by an interactive online workshop to ask questions has been popular with farmers.

To make use of the IfA live platform, follow the steps below: 

1. Contact Innovation for Agriculture (info@i4agri.org) to discuss details of the event you'd like to host on IfA Live.
2. Provide Innovation for Agriculture with the digital materials you would like uploaded to the platform.
3. Stipulate how long these resources should be available for, and who should have access (open or restricted)

description.title: Opt-In is a collection of courses and events aimed at helping rural dwellers find the means and skills necessary to increase their income generating options.
description.description:
The Opt-In web portal is an innovative communication tool linking the services provided by the various skills and training stakeholders to rural dwellers.  The objective of the website is to provide a portal for rural communities to enable the generation of new employment and diversification opportunities. The portal acts as a complementary tool to the dissemination methods already used by training providers. It provides information in relation to the different dimensions necessary to generate alternative income generating opportunities.

•The service providers update the calendar page on a monthly or yearly basis with details of planned activities which can be linked back to a stakeholder website. The Opt-In portal has been developed in a user-friendly manner for partner agencies uploading content.

•While the primary objective of Opt-In will be to present information digitally in a visually attractive format, the web interface has been designed so as to also facilitate the printing of the calendar on the Opt-In website as a poster which can be posted in partner agency offices. This allows the various agencies to see what’s happening within their functional area and can help cross fertilise the sharing of information between agencies that would otherwise not have any level of contact.

•The system encompasses inbuilt monitoring and reporting on the uploading of content by partner agencies. This will mean that when the general public registers on Opt-In they will choose preference skill or training areas within which they express an interest. This will result in the public being notified by social media channels such as Twitter, Facebook, E Mail or text when and where a course is taking place on a subject they expressed an interest in on registration.

•The web portal comprises of: Dynamic Content containing regularly updated content in calendar format re skills training, diversification and business development opportunities. Static Content containing advice for farm families in relation to skills development, business development and diversification opportunities. Both dynamic and static content can be provided by any stakeholder.

The portal was developed as part of the Teagasc Options Plus programme which partners with external agencies that provide a range of courses and information to meet the skills needs of rural dwellers to help them access the labour market or supplement family income through diversification opportunities.

description.name: Calculate carcass growth per kg of feed consumed - Karkasgroei per kg voeder
description.title: Web tool for pig farmers to calculate carcass growth per kg feed and standardized carcass growth per kg feed.
description.description: The calculation formula is based on two different parameters and corresponding formulas: carcass growth per kg feed and standardised carcass growth per kg feed. Both place weight gain in the counter and feed use in the denominator, in contrast to most existing feed conversion formulas. In this way, the parameters better match conventional productivity parameters, such as labour or capital productivity. In addition, it was decided to work with warm carcass weight instead of live weight, as this is measured by default in Flemish slaughterhouses. Our neighbouring countries and most other European countries also work with warm carcass weight. Carcass growth per kg feed serves as an objective representation of the performance of the fattening pigs in a production round. The standardised carcass growth per kg feed corrects for mortality (using a linear model) and is representative for a weight trajectory of 25-115 kg (live weight). The latter formula makes comparisons between farms much simpler and unambiguous.

description.name: DjustConnect
description.title: Data sharing platform. DjustConnect provides a digital infrastructure for safe data transport.
description.description:
Imagine it as a highway, with on-ramps for data providers and off-ramps for data receivers. At each on-ramp there’s a barrier operated by the farmer. Only when they open the barrier DjustConnect delivers the requested data package to the correct receiver. We don’t open the package and guarantee safe transport from start to finish. 
Someone has an idea for a new app or tool and is looking for data to develop this. In the ConnectShop from DjustConnect they can view the different data sources on offer and register as data receiver. They choose the data they need and with a click their application will end up with the data provider.
The data provider approves the idea. Using the DjustConnect dashboard they give permission for the use of their data by the data receiver. This seals the deal legally as well. However, the farmer will need to give permission before the data is transported. 
The farmer reviews the data request in their personal DjustConnect dashboard. If they agree, the data transport starts. The farmer can stop this transport at any time with one click in his dashboard.
The new app or tool is ready. The app combines the data in a smart way. This improves the advice the app user gets or helps to ease the burden of administration.

description.description:

Developed in Javascript and HTML5, it does not need previous installation in the client machine.

For the correct execution of the viewer the browser must comply with the HTML5, CSS3, JS standard.

The viewer comprises several groups of raster layers and a significant number of vectorial layers:

-Layers

oMap (MTN 1:2,000,000, MTN 1:1,250,000, MTN 1:500,000, MTN 1:200,000, MTN 1:50,000, MTN 1:25,000)

oImage (Sentinel 2, Orthophotos PNOA)

oStreet map Cartociudad

oRelief (DTM 1:200,000, DTM 1:25,000)

oFloor Occupation (Corine , SIOSE)

Historical Maps



-Additional layers

oAdministrative limits

Mapping Grids

oElevation

oHistorical PNOA

oUser files (GPS, KML,Shp, GeoJSON)



Similarly, raster layers from WMS/WMTS services can be displayed as well as vector layers in different formats (KML,GPX,Shape,GML, GeoJSON)

The desired areas can be displayed at any time at different scales (zoom in or out). Depending on the scale of the screen display, one or other layers of each group will be shown.

The application also offers, among others, visualization tools (image scrolling, zoom in on the peninsula and the Canary Islands, etc.), tools for measuring surfaces and distances, search, attribute querying and printing.

description.name: Fertiliser relative values online calculator
description.title: Relative Value of chemical Fertilisers to help farmers choose low cost fertiliser blends and compounds (Using Urea)
description.description:
This online calculator allows advisers and farmers compare the relative value of NPK fertiliser blends and compounds at any point in time based on the comparable price of three of the main fertilizers on the market. The primary components of fertilizers are nitrogen (N), phosphorous (P) and potassium (K). This program calculates the relative cost of a unit of nitrogen, phosphorus and potassium and using this information calculates the value of other compound fertilizers, depending on the proportion of each component in it. I should be noted that the calculator uses elemental N, P and K values expressed as % of the mixture. (some compound refer to % p2o5 which you convert by multiplying by 0.437 also K is often referred to as k2o which can be converted by multiplying by 0.83)
The input sheet consists of a table of the main fertilizers available on the market, their component analysis and current price. Additional fertilisers formulations can be added. Fertiliser prices marked with an asterisk must be entered for the calculation to be done.
The calculator uses two NPK compound fertiilisers (0-10-20 and 0-7-30) which are common on the Irish market to calculate the value of the P and K component. It has two versions which allow you to use Urea (46%N) or Calcium Ammonium Nitrate (27%N) to calculate the value of the N component.
The practical application of this calculator is to help farmers choose the best value combination of fertilisers when buying their required fertilisers and matching these to the crop/soil needs. All an advisor needs is a list of fertiliser types available and a corresponding price list. 
An MS Excel Version of this calculator is avaliable for offline use.

description.name: DigiCompost
description.title: Digitising organic compost production
description.description:
Organic compost is a nutrient-dense, cost-efficient natural fertiliser which supports soil regeneration and the increase of soil organic matter. In Greece and many other countries in the region, due to cost limitations and/or lack of digital skills, the manufacturing of compost, which is taking place in open space piles, is mainly manual. This includes both the monitoring of the compost conditions, which is limited to manual daily checks of the pile’s temperature, but also the decision making and management process, including compost irrigation and stirring activities. 
DigiCompost is a solution that digitises the overall production process, offering extensive supervision, control and optimisation over it along with prediction capabilities on the compost’s nutrients and time of completion. DigiCompost solutions integrates a number of state-of-the-art yet mature technologies: Sensors monitoring the compost pile’s conditions (Temperature, Moisture, pH, O2), IoT technologies for data collection and persistence (wrapped around LoRaWAN), Data analytics (including Machine Learning and AI that allow the identification of correlations among monitored parameters and compost process/outcomes) and Smart Modeling that supports the decision making for automated actuation (compost sprinkler activation when temperature and humidity are above/below a threshold) or dynamic notifications for actions (e.g. usage of compost turner to aerate the compost). DigiCompost supports the concurrent management of multiple piles of large volumes of heterogeneous organic raw materials that are composted in open spaces. At the same time relevant restrictions are being considered are to keep the environmental footprint, the financial investment cost and overall nuisance level to minimum. In order to monitor critical metrics in the field, i.e. parameters related to compost conditions, a technical infrastructure which incorporates and combines sensor devices for recording metrics, RF network for data transmission and a light IT infrastructure for data persistent storage and visualisation, is being used.

description.name: ForestSIM
description.title: ForestSIM® - Information and Mapping System: An instrument to support the valorization of the forestry sector
description.description:
Certification is a fundamental tool to guarantee a managed and profitable forest with less fire risk. But certification’s systems imply a very complex management of large amounts of information, of different data types (production, cartography, operations, …) and by different actors (advisors, owners, auditors, ...)
ForestSIM® is a tool that helps the certification groups, forest owners and companies to manage all the information required by Sustainable Forest Management certification’s systems.

The ForestSIM® have 4 main components: documents, forms, cartography and planning components. All this information and forms are available online, and advisors and forest owner can fulfill and consult them in the field, or at the office, and confirm the compliance of the Forest Operation Plan and all the necessary requirements to guarantee the future certification.

ForestSIM® was developed to enable the certification of properties by the Forest Certification Systems and its structure allows certification groups and individual entities to manage information more effectively using new technologies, with increased efficiency.
The online tool allows, advisors and forest owners, to have access to:
•	Access to the property limits
•	Forms for the operational control of planed forest activities, with all the Health and Work Safety rules
•	Cartographic editions
•	Maps generated by ForestSIM with legal restriction layers
•	Productivity of forest stands, with models of productivities
•	Management orientations for biodiversity linked to layers of Natura 2000
•	Calculation of biomass and carbon sequestered in the forest 
This tool contributes to improve the work of advisors (cost reducing and better planning) and to help owners to improve forest management by easily accessing to all information as biodiversity, fire prevention management, legal, social, and cultural responsibilities.

description.name: When is my cow no longer profitable ? - Wanneer is mijn koe niet meer rendabel?
description.title: The tool aims to help dairy farmers make decisions as part of their replacement strategy.
description.description:
The tool 'When is my cow no longer profitable' was created by Boerenbond and ILVO. This was done as part of an Operational Group with dairy farmers and with support from the European Agricultural Fund for Rural Development.

The tool aims to help dairy farmers make decisions as part of their replacement strategy. It calculates in a simple way the minimum amount a reformed cow still needs to produce to get out of cost.

In this tool, this is simulated for 2 possible situations: either there is a heifer ready on the farm to replace the reformed cow (= situation 1) or there is no heifer ready (= situation 2). The calculation takes into account the main cost items (ration, manure outlet and labour) and the possible extra meat value.
keywords: Replacement strategy - reform cow - ration cost - manure disposal - labour cost - meat price - standard milk price - milk production heifer

description.name: Web tool for the prediction of Fusarium head blight and deoxynivalenol content, cereal leaf beetles and aphids in wheat - Webtool voor de voorspelling van aarfusarium en deoxynivalenolgehalte, graanbladkevers en bladluizen

consists of two parts. The first part contains information about Fusarium, cereal leaf beetle and aphids. In the second part, the prediction models can be consulted. After logging in, the farmer can enter cultivation details for each parcel. On the basis of these data combined with  the weather conditions of the nearest measuring point of the Soil Service of Belgium, several predictions can be requested during the growing season.

description.name: Kialo
description.title: Kialo (kialo.com) is a public discussion platform designed to facilitate reasoned debates about complex topics online.
description.description:
Kialo describes itself as “a debate platform powered by reason” to encourage clear and concise arguments from both sides.  It is a public discussion platform designed to facilitate reasoned and civilised debates about complex topics online. It launched in 2017 and has grown into a community with thousands of debates and millions of contributions. Kialo is a purpose-built tool for critical thinking, thoughtful discussion, and collaborative decision-making. It works far better than meetings and lengthy email chains, or debates on social media platforms. Discussions are presented in structured argument “trees” that allow others to understand quickly why a decision was made, and who was swayed which way.

Users find themselves confronted by a series of opinionated statements; i.e. such as “Digital technology will reform agriculture” which is accompanied by an image. These are the site’s theses or the issues users can debate. Each thesis takes a particular stance on a topic, thus inviting users to respond with claims that support the stance or disagree with it by inputting pros and/or cons to the statement. There is a ranking system for contributors to vote on claims and counterclaims, but the aim is to judge their impact on the claim, not on their amusement or outrage.

By clicking on a thesis or its accompanying image users are taken to a page that shows the full thesis and a circular infographic. These infographics are one of Kialo’s proprietary design features: dual-colored visual representations of what the site calls the “discussion typology.” Each discussion automatically tracks and collates certain information about the discussion. A coloured circle with linear stalks radiating from it in all directions represents the evolution of a discussion. Kialo is particularly useful for advisors during Covid-19 to facilitate group work with clients.

description.name: Organic farm knowledge
description.title: Organic farm knowledge - A unique resource with tools for farmers across Europe
description.description:
The online platform Organic Farm Knowledge intends to promote productivity and quality in organic farming across Europe by providing access to a wide range of tools and knowledge exchange among farmers, farm advisers and scientists. FiBL maintains the platform in collaboration with partners from the OK-Net Ecofeed project. The core of the platform is the "toolbox". Available tools include, for instance, practical tips, factsheets, guides, online calculation tools and videos. By promoting the exchange of good practices, it supports farmers and advisors in putting innovations into practice.

Topics covered on the platform cover a wide range of knowledge about organic farming, from arable crops, soil quality and fertility, nutrient management, pest and disease control to pigs, broilers, laying hens, feeding and ration planning and the handling of harvested feed. This facilitates access to practical tools developed within the framework of research projects with farmers playing a central role in building the platform from its roots.

-How can I find a tool?
For each of the themes of Organic Farm Knowledge, a dedicated theme page has been set up where the tools related to a theme can be found along with some background information. An extensive search function is also available, and tools can be browsed by theme, keyword, tool type, country of origin, organisation and language.

-Are the tools available in all languages?
While not all tools are available in all languages, their summaries and key recommendations are translated – via automatic translation – into Bulgarian, Danish, Dutch, Estonian, French, German, Hungarian, Italian, Latvian, Serbian, Spanish and Swedish.

-How can I inform myself about new tools?
All tools are announced on the Organic Farm Knowledge platform with a news item. The latest tools are also highlighted in a separate box on the homepage and on the relevant theme pages. The tools are also promoted via social media: Facebook https://www.facebook.com/organicfarmknowledge, and Twitter https://twitter.com/farm_knowledge.

-Can I rate and comment on a tool or engage in discussions?
Yes, you can rate tools directly on the platform. For discussions, we offer the Organic Farm Knowledge Facebook and Twitter accounts, where each tool is posted and can be commented upon. Also, the discussion function, DISQUS on the platform itself, can be used.

description.name: Agriorisk
description.title: Forecast model for wireworm damage
description.description:
A prediction model for the presence of damage caused by wireworms was developed based on the harmful wireworm species collected and their numbers over the last four years, in combination with the soil parameters and the cultivation history of the plots monitored. The 'random forest' machine learning model proved to be the most reliable and transparent method. Based on the data of 235 plots, ten factors seem to determine the presence of harmful wireworm species. The important parcel parameters are soil type, acidity (pH) and organic matter content. For the plot history, the main crops and the number of years of cereals/grass/maize as the main crop over the past five years seem important. The presence of a green cover in the winter prior to cultivation and the type of tillage carried out one to four years ago may in certain circumstances also have an impact on the occurrence of wireworms.  
Most of the plot parameters and the history of each plot are available through the VLM database (in Flanders) and have been converted into usable map layers for the platform. Any missing model parameters for the intended plot are requested from the user. Linking the predicted wireworm pressure to the main crop of the new season allows the underlying model to calculate the risk of crop damage. The web platform displays a colour code per field, which makes the risk of wireworm damage clear to the grower. An advice is linked to this colour code about the possible measures the grower can take. Among other things, the grower is advised to carry out a sampling on the risk plot. For this purpose, reference is made to the protocol for placing traps.

description.name: AGROgestor
description.title: Digital Platform Ecosystem that provides a specialized technical assistance tool for the Management of Environmental Programs by indicators to support COLLECTIVE MANAGERS of farms with advisory services.
description.description:
The AGROgestor platform offers Collective Management services through Productivity, Efficiency, and Sustainability indicators.
Economic: Production and gross margin.
Water Use: Water consumption, water productivity and Water footprint and its components (blue, green and gray). Water Stress Index.
Nitrogen management: Consumption of total, mineral, and organic N, organic N in relation to total N, N surplus, contribution of N from irrigation water.
Phosphorus management: Consumption of total P2 O5 , mineral and organic P ratio, P2 O5 surplus. 
Emissions balance: Carbon and energy footprint. Use of phytosanitary products:
Number of phytosanitary treatments in a campaign, toxicity in fresh and marine water, terrestrial and human toxicity.
Biodiversity: Different crops in rotation and incorporation of legume crops in the last 4 years.

description.name: Official register of producers and operators of plant protection products (ROPO)
description.title: Official register of producers and operators of plant protection products (ROPO)
description.description:
Official register of producers and operators of plant protection products (ROPO) 

 In accordance with article 43.5 of Royal Decree 1311/2012 of 14 September, which establishes the framework for action to achieve sustainable use of plant protection products, MAPAMA has created a computer application for the exchange of information on entries in the Official Register of Producers and Operators (ROPO).

This application allows public consultation of registered entities and operators, with a double purpose, on the one hand to inform the applicants of the status of the application in real time, and on the other hand to inform the general public of the qualification of a specific operator or entity to carry out any activity related to the marketing, distribution and use of any means of phytosanitary defence.

description.name: Mapping paddocks using Google My Maps
description.title: This is a digital mappling service for advisors to help farmers in the design and management of controlled grazing of livestock at pasture
description.description:
The DATS uses Google MY Maps to provide a professional advisory service to farmers enabling them to divide up their grazing areas accurately. The service is provided by many advisors to their clients and is supported by the buildings and machinery specialist. Links are provided to supporting documents and there are 3 Youtube training videos.
Please note while google MYmaps is free to individual users, however you should check if the application of the Mymaps tool is a service which requires an enterprise licence to use.

description.name: The SigPac Viewer
description.title: The SigPac Viewer is a web application that aims to visualize the data of the "Geographic Information System for the Identification of Agricultural Plots" (SIGPAC).
description.description:
The application is connected via Internet with:
SIGPAC Data Servers, which provide the vectorial graphic information (plots, enclosures...) and the associated alphanumeric data (uses, surface...).
Image Server, which provides the raster images:
Maps of the National Geographic Institute of the available areas, with information corresponding to roads, sites, mileage points, etc.
Orthophotographs of recent flights made for SIGPAC.
At any time it is possible to visualize the desired areas at different scales (zoom in or out). Depending on the scale of visualization on screen, the orthophoto and/or the topographic map of the National Geographic Institute is shown in the background and, superimposed on this image, the corresponding vectorial data.

The application also offers, among other things, visualization tools (scrolling through the image, zooming to the initial extension,...), surface and distance measurement tools, search, attribute querying and printing.

description.name: e.GEBA
description.title: Farm Management Programme
description.description:
“e-GEBA” is a computer-based farm management programme developed by the Lithuanian Agricultural Advisory Service (LAAS) following years of experience, international best practice, and recommendations from Lithuanian scientists. The programme consists of four modules covering all farm activities: 
1.	Animal husbandry, 
2.	Economy, 
3.	Bookkeeping, 
4.	Plant production.
Separate, but also interconnected, modules of “e-GEBA” can: collect farm data, plan the farm's activities, manage available stocks, carry out a comparative analysis, evaluate performance, formulate recommendations to improve activities, prepare reports for controlling authorities, automatically receive farm data from external data managers (NPA, AIRBC, etc.). This saves time for advisers and farmers and reduces the likelihood of errors. Having farm data in one place not only allows a quick and objective assessment of a real farm situation, but also identification of the main problems, taking of appropriate actions and planning a long-term perspective for the farm.

description.name: OVIPLAN
description.title: Oviplan, an online tool to help set up a sheep-meat production facility, allows you to situate the means to be implemented in a production context defined by the user,
description.description:
OVIPLAN is an online support tool for the creation of sheep production, intended for all sheep breeders and future sheep breeders. Easy to use, this tool enables everyone, experts or less specialised, to assess the economic impact of a project and its feasibility.

A first simulation to prepare your project
Intuitive, fast and interactive, OVIPLAN allows you to frame your project according to the context of the operation (region, structure, potential...) and the operation of the sheep production (livestock, reproduction, feed, necessary equipment...) and the expected performances. Oviplan uses references from the French livestock farm networks. It allows to build different scenarios and to compare them. It is a first level of response for project feasibility. This simulation will have to be completed by a more complete study carried out by a specialised consultant. 

 A tool for breeders and advisers
 OVIPLAN responds to the request of farmers who are considering a project to set up a sheep production. This includes students and future breeders as well as farmers looking for ways to diversify.
It also concerns advisers looking for a simple tool to present production and the different possible systems of production by region (agricultural advisers, teachers, etc.).

description.name: ROADTool
description.title: Carbon-, water- and waste footprint of agribusinesses
description.description: ROADTool is a web GIS-based app that estimates Greenhouse Gases (GHGs) emissions and water- and waste- footprint at enterprise and at value chain levels. The Tool was developed in the framework of the INTERREG Balkan-Mediterranean project under the title “Towards farms with zero carbon-, waste- and water-footprint. Roadmap for sustainable management strategies for Balkan agricultural sector” and with acronym BalkanROAD (https://balkanroad.eu). The tool estimates the emissions throughout the entire production line of marketable agricultural products, i.e., grapes to wine, olives to olive oil, apples to apple juice, etc, starting from cultivation in the field and ending after packaging and before the market entrance. The user inserts operational data through a user-friendly interface, e.g. fertilizers amount, energy and fuels consumption, water consumption, etc., and receives visualized results.  Moreover, due to its modularity, the ROADtool is easily adjustable to other types of businesses, for example, hotels, storehouses, logistics, etc. Apart from GHGs emissions, the tool estimates the agribusinesses’ water and waste footprint. It is worth noting that the BalkanROAD project which developed the ROADTool was one of the five best projects in Europe in the REGIOSTARS2021 awards in the category SMART EUROPE.

description.name: Agroforestry Planner
description.title: The "Agroforestry Planner" helps farmers, advisers and other applicators draw up a concrete plan for the layout of an agroforestry (forest agriculture) land.
description.description: Agroforestry or forest agriculture is an integrated land use management where trees or shrubs are combined with an agricultural crop or livestock on the same land. In a well-functioning agroforestry system, both components have a neutral or positive influence on each other, and negative interactions are avoided as much as possible through a well-thought-out design. Agroforestry can also provide ecosystem services such as carbon sequestration, shade for livestock or air purification. The Agroforestry Planner guides you step by step in drawing up a plan for planting trees and/or shrubs on your plot. In the first steps, you enter information about your farm, the plot you have in mind, the desired type of agroforestry (trees + grassland or trees + arable or horticulture) and the objectives you want to achieve by planting trees and/or shrubs. You can then plan the spatial arrangement of trees and/or shrubs on your plot and choose an appropriate tree species. The Tree Selection Matrix guides you through that selection in 4 steps, also taking into account the information already entered. Finally, based on the data entered, a summary report is generated which, depending on your choices, also provides specific advice and feedback.

description.name: TITRIS
description.title: The Applied Innovation Research and Results Information System (TITRIS)
description.description:
The Applied Innovation Research and Results Information System (TITRIS) collects, disseminates, and compiles data on applied innovation research and its results that are useful for more efficient, sustainable, and environmentally friendly farming.
TITRIS focuses on research and innovations developed in practice that have, and can have, a greater or lesser impact on the development of sustainable agricultural production. TITRIS system is designed to provide farmers, advisers, and researchers with access to innovations developed in the field of agriculture. 
Research institutions, farms and other specialised producers contribute data on the results of applied research and innovations to TITRIS.
All data is carefully evaluated by competent specialists of the Lithuanian Agricultural Advisory Service before the innovations are made public in TITRIS. The aim is to assess (determine) the clarity, novelty, applicability, and relevance of the information.
TITRIS is a part of the project “Gate of Innovations – the  centre for knowledge accumulation, transfer, development of agricultural technologies and their demonstration”.

description.name: Electronic Record of Transactions and Operations (RETO)
description.title: Electronic recording of transactions and operations (RETO)
description.description:
Electronic recording of transactions and operations (RETO):

In accordance with the provisions of article 25 of Royal Decree 1311/2012 of 14 September, which establishes the framework for action to achieve sustainable use of plant protection products, MAPAMA has created a computer application to facilitate compliance by the sector through the Electronic Record of Transactions and Operations with Plant Protection Products (RETO). This application allows for the registration of information in real time, making it possible to guarantee at all times an integral traceability of the supply chain of phytosanitary products, as well as providing management utilities to the users of the computer application.

To be able to use RETO, the first step that users must do is to proceed to SELF-REGISTRATION in the application. The operator who intends to use RETO must be previously registered in the Official Register of Producers and Operators of Phytosanitary Products (ROPO), regulated in chapter X of Royal Decree 1311/2012, of September 14, in the supply sector or in the treatment sector.

description.name: The Cool Farm Tool
description.title: The Cool Farm Tool is a web-based software tool that incorporates environmental impact metrics covering greenhouse gases (GHGs), water, biodiversity and waste.
description.description:
The CFT is a global tool designed to be easily completed and to calculate GHG emissions based on information a farmer has available. Assessments are normally made for a specific product and reference period, while impacts on biodiversity are assessed for the whole farm.

The different modules are divided into sections with multiple questions about the farm practices and the crop or animals you are assessing. Much of the requested data just requires general knowledge of the farm, herd and agricultural practices. This can be supported by additional information such as energy bills, feed or input records.

For GHG footprints, the result is a summary of emissions sources and sets the basis for decisions and management practices a farmer could implement to create a positive impact on total net greenhouse gas emissions.

The methodology of the Cool Farm Tool is based on leading academic research (conducted amongst others by the Universities of Aberdeen, Cambridge, Edinburgh and Wageningen) and includes a broad range of published data sets and IPCC methods.

The tool is free for farmers to use, for companies there is an annual cost.

description.name: VitiGEOSS platform - Intelligent services for smart vineyard management
description.title: Innovative vineyard management platform
description.description:
Innovative vineyard management platform providing forecasts, estimations, and recommendations for winemakers to help boost vineyards’ sustainability and adaptation to climate change. The platform offers tools for supporting farmers and business managers to improve their decision-making processes, and, thus, the optimisation of vineyard operations. 

The intelligent services offered combine the Internet of Things, Earth Observation services, sensors, mathematics, and Artificial Intelligence to provide winemakers with complete information about climate, phenology, plant physiology, and disease forecasts, as well as management recommendations for their business operations. 

The platform comprises a wide range of digital decision-support services for viticulture, including weather, sub-seasonal and seasonal climate predictions, and a service that offers monitoring and forecasting of vineyards’ phenology. This service utilizes satellite and in-field observations to better plan and organize the management of the vineyard. 

The system is also prepared to provide useful information on actual crop water consumption and actual biomass production of grapevines, among other vegetative variables. Through this service, farmers can receive water consumption demand and yield estimates for the upcoming season. Other services available in the platform comprise a service that provides predictions of the risk of downy and powdery mildew infections, as well as suggested treatment recommendations to be applied, adjusted to the risk level.

The VitiGEOSS platform also aims to reduce exploitation costs and the environmental impact of operations in the wine sector. In that regard, a business management service has been developed to manage field tasks, such as harvesting, taking into consideration the economic costs, environmental impact, and the timing available.

description.name: Klimrek climate scan - Klimrek klimaatscan
description.title: The climate scan is a company-specific screening tool, revealing where the focal points of the company's climate impact lie.
description.description:
Klimrek is a VLAIO agriculture project in which ILVO, Boerenbond and VITO work together on a climate trajectory for dairy farmers, pig farmers, and arable farmers with potatoes in the cropping plan.
To this end, a climate consultant 'goes out on the farm' with a targeted climate scan and climate course to support farmers in switching to a more climate-friendly and climate-robust business management, also taking into account practical and economic feasibility.
A climate scan based on a life-cycle analysis (LCA) is developed per sector, with which the climate consultant visits the farmer to collect the necessary data.
That climate scan is a company-specific screening tool, which makes clear where the focal points in the company's climate impact lie. With that insight, the consultant can select climate measures tailored to the farm. The consultant then visits the company to present and discuss these measures with calculated scenarios. At the same time, a cost-benefit analysis is drawn up to estimate the economic feasibility.

description.name: IoT Catalogue
description.title: IoT Catalogue: BRINGING IoT USERS AND TECHNOLOGY PROVIDERS TOGETHER
description.description:
The ‘IoT Catalogue’ aims to be the whole-earth catalogue of the Internet of Things (IoT) - the one-stop-source for IoT innovations and technologies to help IoT stakeholders to take the most advantage of the IoT for the benefit of society, businesses and individuals. The ‘IoT Catalogue’ is thus the hitchhiker's guide for the Internet of Things - a standard repository for knowledge and technology on the Internet of Things.

The ‘IoT Catalogue’ is a web-based catalogue from where to pick & choose IoT solutions; it is an explorer of innovations in IoT applications and technologies. It aims to be single entry point of support to IoT developers/integrators/advisors/end-users in the process of identifying and selecting IoT technologies (ranging from complete end-to-end solutions to tools and components/parts) but also inspecting a wide set of IoT use-cases, their validations, associated contact persons/organisations, detailed characterisation (Value Propositions, ICT Problems, functions, target, domain), supporting technological solutions, and much more.

A key purpose of the ‘IoT Catalogue’ is enabling users to explore IoT solutions based on domain-related Value Propositions and/or ICT Problems described on use-cases defined along applications domains. The ‘IoT Catalogue’ also enables users to inspect solutions and technologies from other domains that might fit to their intents or analyse use-cases similar to their projects thus promoting synergies and reusability between application domains.

IoT Catalogue is a product developed by UNPARALLEL Innovation, Lda. that has received funding from the European Union’s Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme in the following projects: This tool was initialy developed in the context of WAZIUP Research Project, which has received funding from the European Union’s H2020 Programme for research, technological development and demonstration under grant agreement No 687607; IoF2020 - Internet of Food & Farm 2020, funded by the European Commission with the Grant Agreement number 731884, has his Use Case Synergy Analysis Results integrated into the IoT Catalogue; and  CROSSMINER - Developer-Centric Knowledge Mining from Large Open-Source Software Repositories, funded by the European Commission with the Grant Agreement number 732223, adds open-source analysis functionality to IoT-Catalogue.

description.name: Mes Sat'images
description.title: Optimize your nitrogen fertilization thanks to satellite and drone images
description.description:
Optimize your nitrogen fertilization thanks to satellite and drone images
Do you wish to better control the fertility of your soils through precision farming? Optimize your nitrogen fertilization on wheat, barley and rapeseed? Our decision support tool (OAD Fertilization) accessible from MesParcelles service platform has been developed to allow you to make the right decision at the right time and in the right place.
How does the tool work?
Using satellite or drone imagery, you can obtain a map of the biomass of your plots in order to follow the evolution of the state of your crops.
The resulting recommendation map informs you about the yield potential of your crops and the right amount of nitrogen fertilizer to use, taking into account the heterogeneity of your plots. You can thus adjust the nitrogen fertilization during the season in relation to the planned balance sheet dose to achieve your yield and quality objectives.
Precise and tailor-made maps!
- The maps are accessible in digital version from your smartphone or tablet and can be valorized in MesParcelles in the fertilization management module.
- The modulation cards provided as part of the service can be inserted on your console and will allow you to modulate your nitrogenous fertilization to the nearest square meter.
- The console provides you with an application map allowing you to compare actual and forecast interventions.
- Throughout the process, an advisor from the Chamber of Agriculture will provide you with agronomic expertise to help you interpret the maps in order to best adjust your fertilizer intake to the situation.
- A hotline is available and an advisor can visit your farm by appointment.
- The imaging models used are tested and certified by our network of agricultural experts at the Chambers of Agriculture.
Data acquisition chain with the OAD Fertilization of MesParcelles
1- Ordering the online service from the platform MesParcelles. For the plots to be analyzed, acquisition of satellite imagery or processing drone. 
2- Automatic registration of the recommendation map in the Fertilization module of MesParcelles. 
3- Interpretation and possibility of adjustment of the data by the farmer (autonomous action or accompanied by your adviser). 
4- Transfer of the modulation card in your console for an automatic modulation of the field interventions. 
5- Application map of the actual interventions compared to the forecast.

description.name: Metadata Catalogue
description.title: The Metadata Catalogue is part of one of the three fundamental services that must exist in a Spatial Data Infrastructure (SDI), along with Downloads and Map Viewers.
description.description:
The metadata catalogue contains the metadata (data description) of the resources and services offered by the Ministry of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food (MAPA) and the Ministry for the Ecological Transition and the Demographic Challenge. Through this catalogue, users will be able to carry out searches, through options such as alphanumeric criteria, geographical scope, thematic category or area of activity, additionally offering a reference map to interact with the user and facilitate the search for information, as well as visualize the maps and obtain geographical information.
We consider that the data should be exploited and reused by other users to allow us to advance in the knowledge of our projects. That is why we make some of the georeferenced data available to users through interoperable map services, implementing the OGC specifications for Web Map Services (WMS), Web Feature Services (WFS) and Web Coverage Services (WCS).

With the help of the "Organic Conversion Planner" you can roughly estimate what this would mean for your farm. description.name: PrHo V.2.0
description.title: A decision support system for irrigation scheduling of greenhouse vegetable crops
description.description: Software which can be used as a decision support system for irrigation scheduling in greenhouses, in relation to how much and when to irrigate, adjusted to the conditions of Almería (Spain).  It could also be adapted to other areas by previous calibration of the incorporated model. PrHo calculates the irrigation volume based on the estimation of crop evapotranspiration (ETc), which is the sum of the water lost by crop transpiration and soil evaporation. How much water to be applied is calculated from solar radiation measured outdoors and the maximum and minimum daily temperature measured inside the greenhouse. In this calculation it is possible to use average climatic data, that is available in a database that comes with the software, or real-time data if that is available. In addition, irrigation can be adapted to the particular growing conditions of each greenhouse, such as plant density, salinity of irrigation water/uniformity coefficient of the irrigation system and the transmissivity of the greenhouse cover to solar radiation (influenced by shading/whitening, greenhouse structure and cladding material).  When to irrigate can be set from user-specified criteria (interval days between irrigations or depending on the amount of water to be applied or the irrigation time) or with the help of tensiometers. The use of this tool helps the grower to optimize irrigation management and to minimize drainage losses in the soil.

description.name: Forest Investment Valuation Estimator (FIVE)
description.title: Forestry Investment Valuation Support Tool
description.description:
The Teagasc Forest Investment Valuation Estimator (FIVE) as a decision-support
tool, using Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis to model indicative financial returns for
forestry land-use options in Ireland. The MS Excel Model provides indicative analysis and decision
support, particularly in relation to reviewing pre-planting options and comparing criteria
such as species, yield classes and rotation lengths according to landowners’ preferences and
objectives.
Potential timber revenues are generated by the FIVE tool through the selection of stand
characteristics and management regimes. A range of variables are used as inputs in a typical
analysis, including species, site productivity, rotation length, relevant premium payments,
establishment and on-going management costs, thinning and clearfell timber volumes and
revenues potentially attainable. The FIVE tool has been used in previous studies of forestry
and agricultural incomes (e.g. Ryan et al., 2014, 2016).
Future cost and revenue streams from forestry are generated in FIVE which are then
discounted to present day values and presented as net present values.
The FIVE tool requires regular updating to ensure input data (e.g. Government forest premium payments) and timber prices are appropriate.

description.name: NEWTON Dictate
description.title: NEWTON Dictate-Solutions for converting speech into text
description.description: With NEWTON Dictate Speech-to-text solution, you can create documents as twice as fast. NEWTON Dictate is a unique solution for writing longer texts and is also suitable for business correspondence. Due to its extensive specialized dictionaries containing professional and technical terms of the profession, the program saves time for users in agriculture, healthcare, administration, and the judiciary. NEWTON Dictate will understand you in an instant! By utilizing neural networks, our technologies ensure a more accurate and significantly faster transcription of dictated texts. BEECAM is associated with analysis software, including the AGATHE software to recognize the insect population density in a given area (field, greenhouse). description.name: RISE (Response-Inducing Sustainability Evaluation)
description.title: RISE – getting sustainability down to earth
description.description:
RISE is an indicator- based method for assessing the economic, social and environmental sustainability performance of agricultural production at the farm level. The purpose of RISE is to contribute to enhancing the sustainability of agricultural production, by supporting knowledge-based processes that are founded on voluntary participation, confidentiality and capacity building. RISE users work in agricultural consultancy, education, in development projects and in raw material sourcing. The steps of a RISE analysis are goal and scope definition, farmer selection and contacting, data collection and interpretation, farmer feedback discussion and reporting.
The RISE method was developed at the School of Agricultural, Forest and Food Sciences (HAFL, www.hafl.bfh.ch), a department of the Bern University of Applied Sciences. Since the year 1999, RISE has been used on more than 6000 farms in around 60 countries worldwide. HAFL and its partner institutions offer training courses, RISE analyses of single farms and farm groups, as well as RISE user licenses and technical support.

description.name: PLANET
description.title: Nutrient management decision support tool encoding official recommendations for UK arable and grassland crops
description.description:
There are several modules in PLANET that carry out different calculation, record keeping and reporting functions. 

      Field-level nutrient planning and record keeping. This module provides recommendations for individual fields that mimic industry standard recommendations (Defras Fertiliser Manual (RB209) in England and Wales; SRUC technical notes in Scotland). Fertiliser recommendations are given for all major nutrients and lime, and for most agricultural and horticultural crops. Recommendations take account of the crop nutrient requirement, the soil nitrogen supply, other soil analysis results, and the nutrients supplied from any organic manure applications. A nutrient application plan can be developed and updated during the season. Detailed records can be kept of cropping, soil analyses, and each actual fertiliser and manure application. Reports can be produced.
      The Livestock Manure N Farm Limit. This module calculates the manure N capacity and the manure N loading of both derogated and un-derogated farms. Compliance with the Livestock Manure N Farm Limit is required by all farms within an NVZ.
      Organic manures inventory and the NVZ Minimum manure storage requirement.  The Inventory calculates the quantities of slurry, dirty water, poultry layer manure, poultry litter and other solid manure produced on a farm and their NPK content. The module also calculates the minimum slurry and poultry manure storage requirement of a farm in order to meet the requirements of the NVZ regulations.
      Existing manure storage capacity. This module calculates the capacity of existing slurry, dirty water and solid manure stores.
      The N max limit. This module calculates compliance with the N max limit for individual crop types on the farm, grown on land in an NVZ. Compliance with the N max limit is required by the NVZ regulations.
      Farmgate nutrient balance. This module calculates the balance (i.e. surplus or deficit) of nitrogen, phosphate and potash for the whole farm. The balance results from calculation of nutrients imported onto the farm (e.g. in fertilisers, feedstuffs, organic manures) compared to the nutrients exported off the farm (e.g. in crop and livestock produce, organic manures). For each nutrient, the calculated balance is compared with typical benchmark values for the farm type.

description.name: Agrirouter
description.title: The agrirouter is a universal data exchange platform for farmers and advisors that makes it possible to connect machinery and agricultural software, regardless of manufacturer.
description.description:
The data exchange platform solves a fundamental problem in the digitalisation of agriculture: It enables farmers and agricultural contractors to exchange data between machinery and agricultural software applications from a wide variety of vendors.

Each user can create a personal agrirouter account and creates their own data stream consisting of his individual machinery and management systems. In the control centre, only they can determine the routes through which his data is transmitted. The user decides which manufacturers, service providers and consultants have access to his personal data. The data is released on a data record basis and not as a fundamental principle. agrirouter transfers the data, but does not save it. In this way, the user retains full control over their personal data and only they are able to send the data to the respective experts. The agrirouter simplifies the exchange of data and therefore operational processes. It reduces administration time and improves profitability.

Existing or new machines from all manufacturers can also be connected to an agrirouter account via telemetry connections available on the market.

description.name: SustainFARM Public Goods Tool
description.title: SustainFARM Public Goods Tool
description.description:
The SustainFARM Public Goods Tool is a sustainability assessment for farmers that combine both food (crops, livestock, vegetables, fruit olives) and non-food (timber and wood fuel) production. 
The assessment takes a broad approach, using information that a farmer would have available to assess the sustainability of their farming system over a 12-month period. Farms are assessed on several areas, referred to as ‘spurs’, that may be impacted by agricultural management practices and are related to public goods such as water quality, air quality, etc.

These spurs are:
- Soil management
- Landscape and heritage
- NPK balance 
- Energy and carbon 
- Food security 
- Agricultural systems diversity 
- Social capital 
- Farm business resilience 
- Animal health and welfare management
- Governance

Each spur is assessed by asking questions based on a number of key “activities”. Answers are scored on a scale between 1 (poor) and 5 (excellent) and an overall score given for each spur. Results are captured on a radar diagram to give an instant visual overview of the sustainability of the farm.

Farmers can also use the tool to investigate the effects of changing or introducing new practices or management to the farm on the sustainability indicators.

description.name: Teagasc Biomass Calculator
description.title: Teagasc has developed an Excel Calculator which calculates the net cash flow over a 20 year investment period in willow and miscanthus.
description.description:
Crops such as willow and miscanthus have a role to play in providing energy as well as reducing greenhouse gas emissions. However, these crops are difficult to evaluate economically compared with more conventional farming enterprises. For instance, willow is planted only once and is harvested only every two or three years in a 20-year plus cycle. Furthermore, willow is an expensive crop to establish. A breakdown of operational and materials costs involved in the establishment of a biomass crops is therefore extremely important. There are numerous assumptions which can be made. Teagasc has developed a spread sheet which can determine the annual net cash flow based on such assumptions which include the following variables:
- Bank interest rate
- General inflation rate
- Energy inflation rate
- Not VAT registered
- Initial establishment costs borrowed over 20 years
- No willow or miscanthus gate fee for sludge
- No carbon premium
- 50% of the establishment costs covered by grant aid
- Annual Land Opportunity Cost (OC)/ha

 The spreadsheet can be accessed by a farmer through their local advisor.

The medicines used on the farm can be entered into the calculator and the products will be converted into commonly used measurements of antimicrobial use (mg/Population Correction Unit (PCU) – PCU and Defined Daily Dose – DDD). Use of highest priority critically important antimicrobials (CIAs) can also be highlighted.

description.name: CostCheck: The Mastitis Cost Calculator
description.title: The objective of the CostCheck calculator is to allow each dairy farmer to estimate the potential gains in profit from reducing the incidence of mastitis on his/her dairy farm
description.description:
This tool was developed by Teagasc researchers in consultation with the CellCheck Technical Working Group.

The CostCheck calculator is a Microsoft Excel based tool which has a user friendly interface and contains both a ready-reckoner and detailed calculation as options. The calculator can be used by farmers or advisors to help understand the impact that mastitis has on profitability on a farm. The CostCheck calculator is easy to use and will provide an instant cost associated with mastitis on the farm and should be used as a catalyst to the introduction of mastitis control programs.

Estimating the current cost of mastitis on Irish dairy farms is the first practical step in motivating farmers to improve mastitis control, thus enhancing milk quality and farm profitability.  Users of the tool have a choice of using either the Quick CostCheck calculator or the Complete CostCheck calculator. The Quick calculator requires only two inputs and provides an estimate of the impact of mastitis on net farm profit; the Complete CostCheck calculator allows the user to
input more data for a farm specific analysis.

On selecting the Quick calculator this screen is displayed. This is a short cost of mastitis calculator, whereby the only data entered by the user is the number of cows they are milking and the most recent annualised SCC category of their dairy farm (as reported in your milk statement). The calculator then returns an estimate of the reduction in net farm profit due to mastitis on the user’s dairy farm. If the user wants a more detailed analysis then he/she can select the Complete CostCheck button and this will bring the user back to the opening screen.

On selecting the Complete CostCheck calculator the user will see a screen where numerous inputs are required. The results are split into two sections; Summary Results and Detailed Results. The Summary Result provides an estimate of the gain from reducing SCC. The Detailed Results provides a breakdown of the cost components: milk production, treatment, culling and other costs.

It also allows you to sort animals from lowest to highest on their individual  weights and average daily gains.  It produces a bar chart that shows in graph format how a group of animals have performed over a set amount of time.

description.name: FAIRshare Multi-Actor Toolbox
description.title: The FAIRshare Multi-actor toolbox presents the wide range of stakeholder/actor engagement methods and tools available, each with their own (scenario-dependent) advantages.
description.description:
Five core scenarios for multi-actor work were identified and applied in the context of engagement and effective facilitation. The scenarios include:

1. Engaging and incentivising stakeholders and actors, by demonstrating the relevance and usefulness of project events/activities.
2. Interrogating existing knowledge from experts and from static sources such as EIP abstracts.
3. Creating new ideas and knowledge
a. New creative knowledge
b. Co-design of actual processes, products
4. Addressing challenges/problem solving
a. For example creating new solutions, productions, relationships, chains, networks.
5. Applying knowledge to particular contexts, scenarios: application

The identification of these scenarios is useful for the purposes of identifying methods and techniques that can be utilised for each instance. The toolbox documents participatory methods based on each scenario that can be used to be applied to any multi-actor group. The toolbox includes an interactive PDF interface. By clicking on one of the scenarios partners are brought to a Google Sheet where they can view examples of participatory tools/techniques from existing sources. These tools can be used together with stakeholders in achieving specific objectives (i.e. engaging and incensing actors and stakeholders or interrogating existing knowledge etc.). Links are provided to existing manuals and toolkits with instructions how to implement the techniques. Categories of tools/techniques to achieve each of the five objectives are allocated a unique symbol.

description.name: ILVO Farmer Entrepreneur Scan – ILVO Ondernemersscan voor Landbouwers
description.title: ILVO Farmers Entrepreneur Scan
description.description:
To respond to the challenges of sustainable agricultural development it is no longer sufficient for farmers and growers to be a craftsman. Entrepreneurial and management skills are becoming increasingly important. Farmers and growers are thus forced to learn and further professionalize in the field of entrepreneurship. 
A Farmer Entrepreneur Scan was developed to support entrepreneurship in agriculture and horticulture. Intended users are extension workers and study clubs in agriculture and horticulture, where it can be used to guide companies toward sustainable entrepreneurship. In addition to the use as a stand-alone instrument, the scan can be integrated in tools for integrated sustainability assessment. 

The ILVO Farmer Entrepreneur Scan is hierarchically structured according to 8 themes, which are further subdivided into 15 indicators. The 8 themes are:  Vision-Strategy, Planning-Organization-Control, Networking-Collaboration, Risk Management, Opportunity Recognition and Exploitation, Searching and Learning Behavior, Innovation and Craftsmanship. These themes and indicators are visually presented in a radar plot (see figure). The radar plot gives a clear overview of the position of a farm on the level of the entrepreneurial themes and indicators. All scores are shown on a scale of 0 to 100. The width of a segment determines the relative importance of a theme or indicator. A bold black line indicates the mean of a reference group of companies.

description.name: Net Promoter Score (Teagasc)
description.title: Net Promoter Score, or NPS, measures clients experience of the Teagasc advisory service and provides a metric to anchor Customer Relationship Management
description.description:
To gauge the satisfaction of its clients (44,000 in 2020) with its Advisory Service, Teagasc is using a Net Promoter Score-based questionnaire, delivered via SMS (Short Message Service) to its client base.  The SMS contains a link to a short questionnaire.

As a backup, the potential to phone clients for their responses is available for those who can’t respond directly to the SMS for whatever reason. 

The NPS is an alternative to traditional customer satisfaction research.  It is internationally recognised, simple to implement, easy to calculate and comparable over time and locations.  It is commonly used across many industries. It is based on 2 essential questions.  
1) How likely are you to recommend Teagasc to family, colleagues and friends?  Responses are requested on a 10 point scale ranging from ‘not at all likely’ to ‘extremely likely’.
2) A follow on open ended question asks for more detail on the respondent’s choice. 

Answers to question 1) generate an overall score based on the following categorisation. 
- Clients who rate Teagasc 9-10 are ‘promoters’.  They are positive about the service they are receiving and are extremely likely to recommend Teagasc
- Clients who rate Teagasc 7-8 are ‘passively satisfied’.  They are still positive about the service but are unlikely to actively recommend Teagasc and improve its reputation.
- Clients who rate Teagasc 0-6 are ‘detractors’.   They are less positive about the service and who may damage Teagasc through negative word of mouth. 

The NPS is not expressed as a percentage but as an absolute number lying between -100 and +100. For instance, if there are 25% Promoters, 55% Passives and 20% Detractors, the NPS will be +5.
The score is comparable across time and space.  Therefore, it can become a target for action to improve the score.  

Verbatim answers to question 2) provide detail on the score and help focus attention on what needs to change in terms of service delivery in order to improve the score.
